review_text: Great location, accommodation & facilities.

Such a great and well thought out Tourist Park.
Spacious for our group of 7. Location was abit far from everything by foot,but was a respectable drive to the shops.One of us did lose a loved cap that disappeared after housekeeping came around. This person is very particular about their belongings,so they know exactly where they put things

Old cottage … in very bad condition… smelly , toilet not clean properly , dust on the top shelf and back of door , door handles very dirty and sticky marks . Only bed made and carpet vacuum walk area ( not under the bed , next to couch) they need to deep clean once a month . It’s my 2 times experience ..
1- time it was very bad they changed the room but no heating working in cold night .. freeze to death .
2- this time hygiene issue .

They should deep clean and fix toilets , air conditioning, dry cleaners blankets.

Nothing going back
Been here 3 times very cheap not too far of a drive from my house has a pool a soccer court a basketball court and some rentable e scooters

Clean , excellent staff( Eddie at reception was incredibly friendly, courteous and helpful) , great food from the restaurant( the beetroot soup… yup you read it right was superb as was the mushroom soup)… park well managed and the location to get anywhere in Canberra was good… worth a stay ( the only down side was the ENS were dated)
Had a good time here with the family which the park caters for. Will just have to drive to drive 10 mins away to get to anything. It is a little in no mans land.

This is our second time here. The Tourist Park is well set up to cater for families and couples. Surrounded by bush and walking/biking trails brings nature close and enhances the experience. Set on the periphery of the built environment means nothing of significance is extremely close, but also that nothing in Canberra is further than a reasonable drive. Staff at the park were very friendly and helpful. Facilities in cabins show some ageing but are very adequate.
Fire
Great stay , friendly staff , restaurant was amazing
Grosse Anlage mit Pool, alles sauber, ruhig und schön
Very well presented

I've stayed here on a number of occasions with a group of mates, and have previously found it to be great. However, on my most recent stay, an additional charge was applied to my credit card without authority after I'd checked out. After enquiring as to what this was for, I was advised this was an error, and that a refund would be forthcoming. I'm still waiting for the refund, more than a fortnight after my stay and when I followed up I was advised there's nothing could be done. Sadly this means you've lost me as a customer and all for a measly $66.02.
GREAT 2 NIGHT STAY
Kids tell me it's there favourite caravan park.....do I need to say any more 🤔
Great place to bring the family. Plenty for everyone to do and great restaurant and pool area.
Will save you half compared to hotels.

Love Alivio! It’s like a resort with fabulous water activities including spa pools and water play area. It’s in the bush land so the surrounding nature is so calming. The accomodation is great for a range of budgets and needs. Breakfast is fine and coffee has improved since our first visit in 2025. Looking forward to coming back year after year!
Great location. Fantastic water park, dinner and buffet breakfast. Staff were amazing. Our only issue was that the cabin was a bit small for our family of 6. Will book a bigger cabin next time :)

The park itself is very clean
The park is not pram friendly if you want to get around the playgrounds
The pool is huge however no heating and it’s freezing
The food and drinks are great however the cleaner does a terrible job the floor in the dining is putrid we soon realised why,
We watched him every  morning Mop the whole floor without cleaning the mop,
Excellent location, centre of everything Canberra has to offer
Over all great place

Firstly, we found the park setting and the child-friendly recreational facilities terrific but, that's where the positives ended.

We stayed in a Garden Villa. It was dirty and I mean, unclean dirty. The tiled floor in the bathroom had revolting stains as did the kitchen and living room floors. The walls and ceilings had marks and holes, the kitchen sink had lettuce and other food scraps left in it. We found chocolate wrappers in the bunk beds and an old sock on the couch. But worst of all for us, we found a previous guest's prescription medication lying on the floor behind the television (having young children, we found this atrocious).

We asked to look at another Garden Villa and found it equally unclean.

We booked three nights over Easter. Our son became ill (which we don't discount as being exposed to the filth in the cabin) so we left early. Effectively we wasted $330 leaving early however, we found staying in the cabin incredibly unpleasant. We took photos of all our complaints.

We noted with interest that previous guests have left equally scathing reviews concerning accommodation cleanliness however, what's very disappointing, management have yet to implement effective measures to ensure ALL the cabins are cleaned properly and maintained to a satisfactory standard.

In conclusion, a great park for kids let down dramatically by cabins that are unclean and poorly maintained.

Amazing views and quiet bush surroundings at a great value! I came with my mother visiting from the states for two days. We shared a two-bedroom space and were both very comfortable. The kitchen is well-equipped and the little things are accounted for like coffee milk and soaps.

We really enjoyed the cabin we had right up the back along the Ridge. We saw so many kangaroo and wild birds that are tolerant of humans taking photos.

There isn't a general store or nearby shops, so get what you need before you arrive. Uber has issues delivering directly to the cabins. We met them at reception.

The park could use signs that let you know where the cabins are on property. Most loops are one-way and that indication would make it easier to manage passage, but overall we had a great stay!

Would come visit again! Very close to Summernats in January, if you can get a spot!

I recently stayed at Alivio Tourism Park and was extremely disappointed with the cleanliness of the accommodation.

When we arrived, the bathroom had hair all over it, there were dirty footprints in the shower, and the bedding had a strange brown stain. I didn't shower in the cabin as a result. The microwave was grubby and the floor felt unclean underfoot and I had to wear shoes. Overall, it did not feel like the room had been properly cleaned at all.

I was staying with my newborn, which made the situation even more upsetting. Instead of feeling comfortable, I felt genuinely disgusted and worried about the hygiene of the space for my baby.

I raised these concerns with the front desk at checkout and was given an email to contact management. I followed this up and sent through photos, but I have not received any response, which is very disappointing.

For a place that should be providing safe and clean accommodation, this fell well below expectations. I would not recommend staying here, I mean sure they have a nice pool and restaurant, however I would avoid, especially for families with young children.

The rooms were actually nice. The food venue games and pool are mint for kids. The issue we had was being surrounded by construction that started up machinery at 630 to 7 am each day even on a Saturday. Once the construction is completed it will be heaps better but in the mean time I would suggest asking for a discount on the adjacent rooms. The price is high for this joint so is kinda BS they didn't offer a discount. Plus in a family friendly place the music the construction workers played was racially motivated abhorrent stuff for kids to overhear. Pretty unprofessional. Music didn't bother me but there were small kids about. Rooms were tidy and in fairly new condition. Staff were friendly as.

Update: After posting this the Housekeeping team did eventually come, certainly did not seem pleased to be here. It was no doubt a harder job than it could have been, turning over a villa with three little boys present is very different to when they are not, oh well.

Nice facilities. Poor housing keeping.

Informed our request for a high chair would be passed to housekeeping however no high chair forthcoming, despite multiple requests (and not informed that this was not possible...).

Cabin fairly clean when we arrived, however found broken glass just under the couch, not ideal when you have a crawling baby.

Asked to clear the beds of personal belongings for sheet change/towel replacement etc on a certain day, did this. House keeping did not attend.

Contacted reception to inform (we would like clean towels and sheets). Apparently it was marked a a completed job... informed that house keeping would come shortly but this did not occur within a reasonable time frame.

When using vacuum cleaner unfortunately it smelled strongly of hot vomit (presumably used previously for vomit), can't be sure but a new bag may have improved this.

Limited supply of toilet paper and bin bags, after being here a few days we needed to use our own, though I guess we could have made another housekeeping request.
Good place but the dishes wasn’t clean in the restaurant

Amazing food. Dinner at The Terminal on-site was delicious with a great selection. Definitely pay for breakfast- food was amazing, and selection was abundant. Great place to stay.

Select a larger stay option if more than 2 people as bunks in the cabins are short - not for tall people.

Our stay at Alivio Tourist Park Canberra was nothing short of incredible—an absolute hidden gem that completely exceeded expectations!

From the moment we arrived, the atmosphere felt relaxed, welcoming, and family-friendly. The staff were genuinely kind and helpful, making check-in smooth and setting the tone for a stress-free getaway.

The accommodation was spotless, modern, and so thoughtfully designed. Everything felt fresh, comfortable, and well-maintained, with all the little extras that make a stay feel easy and enjoyable. It truly felt like a home away from home, surrounded by peaceful greenery that gives you that perfect escape from the busy city.

What really sets Alivio apart are the amazing facilities. The pool area is fantastic—perfect for both relaxing and having fun—and there’s so much to keep everyone entertained. Whether it’s the playgrounds, open spaces, or the overall layout of the park, it’s clear this place has been designed with guests in mind.

The location is also a huge bonus—close enough to all of Canberra’s main attractions, yet tucked away enough to feel calm and quiet. You get the best of both worlds.

Overall, Alivio Tourist Park delivers comfort, value, and a genuinely enjoyable experience. It’s the kind of place you leave already planning your next visit. Highly recommend to anyone visiting Canberra—you won’t be disappointed!❤️

Me and one of my best mates. Had travelled long distances to experience Canberra Raiders play at there home ground and found that our time with checking in to the Alivio Tourist Park.(Canberra) was seamless and inviting. Found all staff to be friendly and welcoming and accommodation to be set a a high standard. Something for everyone can be found staying at this location. A big shout to the manager thanks mate go the raiders
A very comfortable place to stay. I really enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere and the feeling of being close to nature.
Lovely place, friendly staff best amenities and food in the Cafe on site.

We had an amazing time at Alivio in our caravan, drive through sites are easy and amenities if needed are well maintained and updated.
We had to move to a cabin as there wasnt availability for us one night of our 8 night stay but the staff when booking made it super easy to get us in and out of each set up as quickly as possible.
Eddy one of the reception managers was a legend, we had a power issue on our site (not the parks fault) and Eddy came over and sorted it within a couple of minutes!
All the staff are super friendly and made an effort to connect with our 2 young boys throughout their time in the park!
We had to leave a few days early unexpectedly and the team really made an effort to make that as easy as possible.
We will definately visit if we're ever in Canberra again

Its a nice place to stay. The pool area is great. The restaurant set up is good. We cant eat there because of allergies but its a nice set up for those who can.
There is beautiful wildlife around, easy access to everything around.
The reception staff are friendly and helpful. But the maintenance house keeping team... hmmmm... they drive around far to fast or park their vans in the middle of the road and your just left waiting... one older man , shaved type head, was grumping at another employee,  as we were walking by, then sped around a corner n had to slam his breaks on and then tooted at us but were just walking to the pool doing nothing wrong. He was the one who got a fright doing the wrong thing. was actually quite disappointing.
But the accommodation is nice, lots of space, different accommodation to suit budget. The parking is tricky though, so if you have a big car id suggest mentioning it to them.
Booked a beautiful 3 brm villa for sons family ,2brm for myself and hubby . Ours was nothing like photos  it was pokey , shabby , dirty . Not happy .

We have stayed here that last 2 years in January  with our young children. We have enjoyed each stay.
Our cabins have been great, plenty of room, and were clean and comfortable.
The pool, verandah and restaurant are great for families. Each afternoon we would explore the grounds with plenty to keep our kids occupied.
We have found staff to be friendly and helpful.
A stay in Canberra but feels like you’re at the beach. We’ll be back again next year.

I recently visited this place, highly recommended it is next to Canberra city, and can't imagine ever it is middle of a jungle, surrounded by trees, with birds sounds, for nature lovers best place. Very clean ...
Luxury villas, Garden Villas, cabins fir van and caravan visitors,  Bar and restaurants available on site...
Very convenient place...
Highly recommended place for nature loving tourist....

Stayed three nights at the Alivio campground in O’Connor (a suburb of Canberra).

Although the caravans were close to each other, the area is great because it has ensuite for caravans and you could eat at the restaurant/bar in the main building. We had great meals in the restaurant which consisted of a mocktail, plate of oysters and a lovely well cooked steak.There were barbecues available. If you wanted to cook your own the facilities were great with the pool and activity centres, which included a half basketball court. You definitely couldn’t be bored at this site.

The birds were very tame and the parrots came up very close to you in expectation of something to eat, so we gave them some birdseed.

If you didn’t want to use a caravan there was also cabins available.

A great place to stay while visiting in Canberra.
Booked an ensuite caravan site, ensuite was dirty and cleaning equipment placed in the corner of toilet which was disgusting, went to use the mop and it fell apart and was filthy.
Great park clean lovely pool & amenities spotless
Great place to stay whether its family or a boy weekend away

A great place to stay. The villa is so comfortable and homely. The restaurant is divine and they make a great pina colada. The pool is terrific and the staff are so so nice. The best place I’ve stayed in a long while.

Stayed here in our van previously and couldn't speak highly enough of the facilities. Stayed in a cabin just one night. No remote for one tv 2nd tv remote flat .No coffee no detergent to wash up both bedside lights unplugged. Surely these should be checked when cleaned.When I told staff they were very blazay about it and just said oh sometimes these things get missed .
Will look elsewhere next time such a shame as our first experience was so good .
Can't speak highly enough of the kitchen and staff there.
Brilliant, location, amenities, service. Highly recommed.
캠퍼밴을 가지고 ensuite 이용했는데 매우 좋음. 시설 넓고 깨끗하고 수영장도 커서 성인도 이용하는데 어려움이 없음. veranda에서 저녁도 먹었는데 다른 음식점과 비슷한 수준의 가격이 나옴. 음식하나에 20~25달러수준. 음식 맛은 괜찮음

I have stayed at the Alivio multiple times and always been happy. However recently I stayed there with my mum and my two kids during the 40 degree weather. We didn't arrive at our room until the evening. The temperature outside was still 40 degrees. Our room was completely shut up and was like a hot box we put the aircon  on flat out. By the time my kids went to bed it was still far too hot. So three of us slept on the couch and put mattress' on the floor in the lounge room under the aircon. The fridge was also completely turned down which we didn't realise until the following day. So the meat I had purchased had to be thrown away. On day two the room was still sitting at 27 degrees in the lounge room despite the aircon being set to 16 degrees since the night before. The Bedrooms were hotter. I decided to check the filters in the aircon which were absolutely filthy so I cleaned them. This very uncomfortable stay could have been avoided with the aircon being put on earlier by staff and a bit of basic cleaning and maintenance. The fridge temperature should have been checked by cleaning staff.

What an amazing place even though it was only my husband and I, I would highly recommended this place if you also have children ,the outdoor sports they have basketball court ,tennis court , table tennis swimming pools is nothing but amazing to keep the kids busy .
looking forward to returning and can highly recommend this beautiful place

We arrived to our room in which the cleaners had left all used linen bags and rubbish on the front verandah and had to call twice to get them taken away. No spare toilet paper in the cupboard, air conditioner was extremely dirty and had the filters removed and were sitting on top of the inside unit. Bathroom door hinge needed to be fixed and also toilet seat was loose these things were fixed the following day but if the cleaners were doing there job properly why are those things not noted and fixed before the next guests arrived.
Immaculate facilities. The onsite restaurant is great and even offers Buffet breakfast. Pool and water play area are also excellent condition. Highly recommended.
Come to Canberra for Australia Day and booked an en-suite site for my caravan. What a beautiful park, the pool, restaurant and facilities are amazing. A great place to stay

We stayed here for 6 days. The cabin was clean enough and well presented. The washing/dryer usage was overpriced at $6 each. We didnt bother with the restaurant as the reviews all said the same. My main annoyance was the service/cleaning staff. Always parking and being in the way along one way streets. We will probably stay here again but not near summernats week.
Very nice place with nice rooms, comfy pool, good restaurant and friendly staff.
good if you have kids

Can't explain how much i was impressed with the facilities, staff, restaurant, splash park, pool, play grounds, breakfast and so much more. This is now our canberra stop. We changed our trip to come back past here. I promise it's a stop you want to make on your big journeys. We will ensure going south includes this place. We love it kids love it 😀
Great place to take the family,
We got a room that was run down and old. Service was great tho, large pool and pretty big hot-tub. Large kiddie pool also and ANOTHER VERY cold 0.8M pool. Thr location is only 10mins approx. from PH

Fantastic Park great for families close to attractions and shopping, Great customer service at reception and also on-site resturant really nice food, Our Cabin was clean and comfortable. Will be definitely coming back to stay in the near future.
Awsome Park, shout out to Eddie, awsome guy friendly and helpful.

Great rooms, great location and great amenities. The staff were lovely.

The coffee was awful. I’m guessing they use cheap beans as it just tasted like milk. We had coffee 3 mornings in a row and the result was the same even though it was three separate baristas.

The buffet was massively overpriced considering it was so basic.

Poor food choices if you have dietary requirements.

Avoid the coffee and buffet breakfast and you’ll have a great time! Everything else was excellent.

Huuuuuge thanks to Eddie, What a legend, made us feel so welcome. You're an asset to the company mate. We will be back every summernats!

Best place to stay in Canberra!
Great place to stay, just a little overpriced for what the villa was like. I would stay again, but I'd have to look at different villas before forking over big money.
Great place to stay with the family food well done

Clean cabins, friendly staff went above and beyond nothing was a problem , secure grounds, close to CBD, good value, fun atmosphere, good areas for kids. Restaurant has a wide variety and lovely food.  Stayed at other places for Summernats previously and Alivio has been the best by far. Will certainly be back next year. Eddy and Katie were very accommodating and very friendly!
Facilities are very good for kids.
Perfect place for a day by the pool, delicious pizzas & great service
Great spot for the kids and not too far away from town

We come back every year. The park is in a fantastic location and close to so many things to do.  The restaurant has a good variety for breakfast and the pizzas are amazing.  The prices are not cheap but the food is worth it.  We really appreciate the pool and play spaces they offer as we are always with our kids. We can highly recommend  the Blue Gum Villas as a stay location as we find the extra money well spent for the space. Awsome bbq areas that we also use on our frequent visits. We love all the staff and especially Eddy they make the park what it is and we appreciate you all. Thanks for continuing enjoyment with our visits, see you all again next year.
Beautiful place and service. Felt like a resort. Was kept super clean and tidy. A few more bins was the needed and that was the only downfall. Will def be back again.
5 stars for the service of the staff and a very relaxing stay.

If you get a cabin they are small and outdated. We have holes in the doors and the previous persons Xmas decorations were still hanging up. For the money I would expect better. The villas are very nice but the cabins are very disappointing
Absolutely brilliant. So much to do. The food was amazing, pools were clean and refreshing, tennis court and basketball court got quite the workout from the family. We will Absolutely be back.
Awesome place to stay in Canberra
Such an amazing place for families! We will definitely be back!

A lovely spot to explore Canberra and enjoy the park's amenities. The pool area and children's playground are very nice. The pitches are quite open and close together; you can hear your neighbors' air conditioning. The roads are narrow, making it difficult to maneuver a large rig; luckily, we only had a camper trailer. The camp kitchen is well-equipped and the amenities clean.

These guys know how to run an accomodation business 👌. We returned last week for another 3 nights with our 3 young grandkids. Whilst I could nitpick on little things like so many others do, this place is pretty well faultless. Clean and spacious cabins with every amenity Great pool, bar and restaurant which is not overpriced. Very well located as only 10-15 mins from mint, zoo and Questacon. Staff were also friendly and helpful. 10/10. Great job guys we’ll be back next year.
Wonderful staff, clean amenities. Location is close but slightly out of the way (I.e. not accessible by public transport but easy via uber or electric scooter).
Stayed in a brand new Boronia Villa. Unreal. Very clean. Great location and price.

Alivio Tourist Park, was a decent place overall. The location was a highlight, fantastic, and accessible to Canberra for visitation desires. I stayed in a double room cabin, which was also well kept for the most part. A few things are to be mentioned however. The room itself had all windows open upon arrival on a 35 degree day, it was completely sweltering, and the bedroom
was inappropriate and frankly
Disgustingly hot to lay down in.

The air con looked very dusty and in need of a service, and honestly only cooled down once the sun set
I do understand, however, that it is hard to maintain cool temperatures in these rooms.

Moreover, the toilet was constantly stuck on flush, which emitted a rather annoying sound. The toilet seat also needed replacing, it looked very old and used. Additionally, the door to
The children room was completely stuck
and not on the hinge, hindering my ability to close it and keep the cool in the lounge area. It was off putting. Otherwise, the room was accompanied by goods such as towels, cloths, plates, a very good industrial setting with perfectly good oven, fridge, stove and microwave. On a side note the air con was very noisy.

The camp site itself was pleasant and kept, and the Pool was also a fantastic facility. I do wish though that the laundry was open a bit later. I wasn't able to clean my clothes after a very busy day, and an early departure for the next day. That all being said, I'd recommend staying here, but would especially recommend it, if those concerns could be remedied.
Heel aardig personeel en alles ziet er verzorgd en schoon uit

I was told to stay here during sport event for my son, but after reading the reviews it's a definite "Hell No"!  Care factor - zero, cleanliness - for the blind, service- pay for manners... thanks reviewers!! Sorry you had to experience unprofessionalism in a industry that should rule in service.
Modern and clean, in a handy location.
Great place for the whole family, amazing facilities, the cabin I stayed probably needed a little more cleaning and the exterior had a year of cob webs but still recommend

Amazing food at the on-site restaurant. the service was quick, friendly, and really helpful. Unfortunately, the condition of our cabin was a bit questionable. When we arrived, the bathroom door was locked from the inside, and although the manager came quickly with his tools to open it. It turned into a bit of a battle with cockroaches during the night, i killed atleast 15 that made it onto our bed.

On the plus side, they do offer 24-hour service, and someone came out right away with bug spray when we called. The beds, however, were quite uncomfortable, two firm single beds pushed together
An incredible spot. Great facilities. Convenient location.
Love this place but could the staff at cafe please use the tamper to ground the coffee into the basket not their hands/ fingers- kind of unhygienic & unprofessional. Cheers ☕️

We were disappointed as the main experience we came away with was that they wanted to squeeze the most money out of guests - but give the least service with poorly fit out cabins.

In the morning before arrival we rang specifically to ask that our room be ready as soon as possible as we needed to get in quickly to prepare to go out for an event that same day.  However even arriving there just before the advertised check in time of 2pm to try to check in quickly, we were told our cabin wasn't ready even by then!  SO we then had to sit around waiting until finally at 2:30pm they could check us into the room.  Their excuse was it is a big park and takes a long time to clean it all.  So if you know that why not hire enough cleaners to be able to do it all in the 4 hours between 10am check outs and specified 2pm check ins, so guests can check in at the advertised time?  It was especially disappointing as we had specifically called ahead to ask!

When we got to the cabin there was a large bag of dirty laundry and a small bag of rubbish from the bin still sitting on the door step which we had to move away just to get in the door.  The bags sat there for several hours and were finally picked up quite a bit later in the day.
Then trying to make some tea or coffee, there were only a very few of the tiny spoon full packets of long life milk. Not even the normal small Long life milk cartons that most parks have.   Walking back to the office to ask for more milk, please, we were told no they didn't have any.

In the cabin itself we turned on the Air Con (which was very hard to get to turn on and off)  and then set it for the temperature we wanted, but it never adjusted in any way.  It just blew out some air, but never cold enough to actually cool the room on a warm day, no matter the setting.  It was either dodgy, or pre-set to not be able to run strong enough to cool the cabin much.

Other things around the cabin of poor quality, or just missing were for example, the curtains had holes in them and didn't close very well.  The dining table was lopsided and didn't sit flat.  The toilet paper was very thin and there was no box of tissues.  Regarding the bottles for use in the shower, the little bottle of shampoo had actually been mostly used up, probably by a previous guest, but it was still left there with the other two bottles and hadn't been replaced with a new full one!   There was also no information folder of what was available there and what might be around the area?
They were pushing us to sign up for the buffet breakfast and to eat there, but you had to register the night before to get the 'discount' price of $21 (still fairly high for brekkie) or if you didn't pay ahead the price was very much more the next morning.  We didn't bother and when we go back to Canberra, it will be to stay somewhere else!
Great place with heaps to do

We really like this park and it facilities,  this is our 3rd year staying here for Stromlo running festival.  Unfortunately our bookings for 2 caravans sites for 3 night , 1 with private ensuite was stuffed up by a male receptionist.  On arrival we discovered only 1 site to be confirmed and no private ensuite.  The young girl on receptionist was very helpful, her superior was not friendly at all. They managed to get us 2 sites together but 1 only 2 nights which were grateful for, but it did spoil our 3 day weekend with friends we hadn't seen for ages.
整体三分，因为客房设施有点旧，但是有小鹦鹉跑到我们房间里来，所以加一星⭐⭐⭐⭐
This is a van park like no other I have stayed in. Incredible facilities. Tennis courts, bar, pizza, amazing amenities. Highly recommend. So close to all attractions.

Stayed with our caravan for 3 nights and thoroughly enjoyed.
Check in was easy and quick by friendly staff.
Getting to sites was a bit difficult as they are tightly packed.
The kitchen amenities were excellent and clean except from 1 lazy user.
The location is great for easy access to the numerous activities in Canberra.
We were too busy to experience the other amenities in the park but there seemed numerous activities especially for the kids.
It seems a very busy and popular place so could be noisy in these times as they do pack in a lot in a limited space, but our trip was quiet and relaxing.
Also plenty of friendly Rosella's that come and say high!
Clean budget friendly cabin, basic beds but for 2 nights it was okay

Visited friends here that were staying.
Beautiful area great pub/restaurant.
Nice environment
I love it here. Great simple but comfy accommodation, a great restaurant and excellent facilities.
We had our campervan so did not stay in rooms or units. Amenities block very clean, hot showers and heat lamps for cold days. Great cafe. Easy drive in to town.
Very small rooms even for only two adults. Excellent location for foreign friends desperate to see kangaroos and they weren't disappointed.

The facilities are great. It's nice having a restaurant on site. The kids loved the pools and splash park and the foosball. So much for the kids to do. The playground for younger kids could be improved. Lots of bird life around as well. We chose the cheapest cabin and it was sufficient for our needs. We enjoyed our stay.

Absolutely amazing staff very clean grounds and amenities the hot tub could be in a better location so the cold pool water don't make it cold other then that this place is unreal. I can't wait to go back I have my new home away from home

Clean spacious cabins, slightly dated interior yet quite comfortable for individuals or families. Similar to many other locations in Canberra, only minutes away from CBD. Make sure to bring warm clothes for winter, but the restaurant on site is going to deliver warm hearty food to surprise you!
Great park, the employees couldn’t do enough for you

Alivio was our chosen accommodation for the Australian Masters games. Perfect for large groups. The facilities were well appointed, clean and in great condition. The rooms were very comfortable and clean. The pool and spa area are excellent as was the restaurant and bar. Would absolutely stay here again.

2 bed cabins were disgusting. Big spiders, multiple cockroaches and terrible beds.
Would not recommend
there was a cat hair in my food and i felt something tickling me while i was sleeping

We’ve just spent 8 days in one of the beautiful blue gum villas, the location is perfect, surrounded by lovely bush land and abundant in wildlife. The park manager was so helpful and kind, really goes above and beyond to ensure all is well. It really is Canberras best kept secret, an absolute Haven with everything you want in a holiday park. Highly recommend, we will definitely be back! Missing it already.

We stayed in the Grevilia Cabin. It was reasonabley clean but needs revamping. Holes in the bedroom carpet. Laminex peeling off the bathroom vanity.We had cockroaches in the cabin. Mattress was hard. Not worth the $230 a night we paid. Staff at reception were lovely and helpful.
We have now stayed here twice, its very central to the town centre. The accommodation is clean and fit for purpose. Facilities such as the pool and sports courts are great for families.

Stayed at Alivio for the Masters Games with my hockey team. Lovely setting. Close to town but in a quiet and natural bush setting. Best thing about the whole place is the staff. Every one of them have been friendly, helpful, polite and accomodating.  Led by example from the front by a hands on and efficient manager Eddie. Thanks Eddie!

Edited to add: we never heard anything from Alivio despite their reply to this review indicating the opposite.

We’re finishing up a stay in a Blue Gum on Friday. The cabin itself is spacious, lovely verandah and wildlife to watch, comfy beds, a bit dirty though with rubbish and pieces of sticky tape on the floor and no soap provided at all.

The Verandah restaurant however we would not recommend. We ordered, waited nearly an hour before a staff member came to say our meals had been ready for 20 minutes and they “couldn’t find us” (we counted 4 other occupied tables at the time..). We did have a buzzer but that didn’t go off. Just as we were bringing the meals to the table, a staff member chased after us saying they weren’t safe to eat and they had to take them back. Then there was a 10 minute back and forth where another staff member said it was probably safe and that if we wanted them remade, we’d go to the back of the queue (for their error!). Not once did we get a ‘sorry’ and all in all, it was an awful dining experience.
Great place to stay. Roomy cabins comfy beds
Great park we had caravan great venue and amazing meals had everything you needed

Had a lovely 3 night stay at Alivio. Its a top park for families with all the activities available. The Club house was a popular easy choice for a meal and beverage at night. We added a buffet breakfast every day to our stay and it was well presented and yum too.
We had a wonderful time.  Our kids booked a cabin, one of the new ones, we had a caravan site.  Onsite restaurant was great!  Staff super friendly and helpful.
Rooms and park were kept clean, convenient location, generally quite and had a free arcade machine
Overall it was a nice stay, although the playground is tiny and meant for babies rather than kids. And installation are small for so many cabins spots.

What a shame, great location and great facilities. The issue with our cabin was that the place was dirty. I could give this a pass as the caravan park was busy and it was a long weekend. The next issue was unacceptable. The room smelt of sewage. We aired it out and thought we had got rid of the smell, then I used the shower in our bathroom. The smell was back worse then even. I went to reception and they mentioned that it sounded like a blocked pipe and that they would inform maintenance. End result was that the problem was never addressed and we couldn't use that bathroom for the whole stay. When checking out we informed them of our dissatisfaction and they responded with some poor excuse. Better to look at other caravan parks that understands how major this issue was.

This is our third time at Alivio this year and our kids don’t want to stay anywhere else! From the amenities- pool, Rec Room, playgrounds, sporting activities, to the great sized rooms, Wifi and services we absolutely love staying here! Our kids love the holiday activities and the manger Eddy always has time for them and puts a smile on their faces! We are definitely going to be back!
Thank you to the entire staff at Alivio!

Great sporting areas to play around in with family and friends, I really enjoyed playing volleyball with class mates at night. As a school group the food was not so great. Made a couple of students lose their appetite with the food being not so great.

All the mattresses in our cabin have giant visable sags in them, the tv was apparently ment to have a built in DVD player for the free DVDs and although there was a bit of a mess around the front desk where extremely helpful sorting this issue and getting us a DVD player and coming to show us how to use it.

Its a bit disappointing the splash park is out of order since it was one of the main reasons we booked to stay here. The kids playgrounds are either too young or too old for all three of my kids. Other people in the cabins around us were very loud and could here their kids running around till and yelling till 11pm which made it difficult to sleep.

The food is such a rip off. Over priced and below average. The breakfast says on the website that it comes with pastries and fresh fruit which there is non. When I asked they said they don't do pastries and then they cut up some watermelon for me as the fresh fruit. It cost $70 for 2 adults 2 children which is ridiculous for cold food buffet style breakfast. You know its crap when the baked beans taste better than everything else.

Dinner was very overpriced and the food was sub par and the alcoholic drinks were also very expensive, for a beer and 3 kids apple juice cost $38. We ordered a dessert with things missing from it. We ordered the lemon meringue wich was very dry and did not come with a Berry compote like advertised.
It's great place to stay with family. Love the setting

Not all rooms are equal, even thought there is a price difference the difference between rooms is quite grand and unexpected. Rooms are nice and warm during the night, kitchens are nice, showers are nice and warm.

I once was on a drive to Canberra,
I zipped to Alivio, oh what a charmer!
At Boronia's lodges,
With EV recharges,
And Nespresso delights that make mornings warmer.Amen.
We stayed in a campsite. Great caravan park, great restaurant, lovely space.

A great location for young families with an on-site pool and lots of Mountain bike trails surrounding the park. The entertainment area has an on-site restaurant as well but the food at the restaurant isn't as good. Unless you can't be bothered ordering Uber Eats or going out, may as well order from there. Regards @fooderarri

Superb place particularly if you wantnto go to Bruce Stadium.
However, there is never enough staff in the bar/restaurant causing long lengthy cues.

Ensuite site. Close to everything

Very nice

This is the worst place I have ever experienced. It was so bad it cost me money. My partner booked with agoda and we paid for it. I gave her my credit card for the deposit and booking as she didnt have hers. When she got there she used my card for the deposit and  only after they refused to give her the room. I was on the plane to qld as I left early and now she was stranded without a room and without money. She was cold and desperate because of this putrid buisness. Thank God the uber driver took pity and drove her to the airport where she had to sleep. The reason the caravan place refused her is because my name was on the card and I was not with her. They thought is was stolen or something but they were the criminals. To this date we have not received a refund. They are crooks. They will steal money. They will not return whats owed. Thats $300 down the drain. My card is not stolen to this date and she is my parter she had and will always have my permission to use it. I hope people dont support this criminal organisation. Until I get my refund and compensation for not being allowed to let my partner use my card and for her to get a room. I will always think of this putrid organisation as criminal.

We wanted a place to stay that had space for our toddler to run around, comfortable cabins, and close enough proximity to activities we wanted to do - Alivio exceeded expectations for us. The Grevilia Cabins were good - just a cabin with two bedrooms, a bathroom and a little common area. But the real highlight was the playground, grassy areas and basketball court - so well laid out, safe for the little guy. It was perfect.

The extra bonus was The Verandah - great space for buffet breakfast ($21 for adults, $5 for an infant if you pre book) and for dinner (reasonable price). There’s space for kids to play in a corner, colouring in, and still it felt like a nice place to be!

We’d stay here again in a heartbeat. Especially in summer to try out the pool and water play area!

Huge caravan/cabin park. Lots of onsight amenities, restaurant and bar, big laundry, we stayed 3 nights in one of their Ridgeview cabins, basic but comfortable. Friendly helpful staff, prices are a bit on the high side but considering the amount of amenities is fair.
Will stay here again.

Just wrapped up a 3 day stay a day earlier, stayed in one of the woodlands cabins, mould behind toilet in bathroom, shower curtain had mould on the bottom, all architraves dusty and multiple stains over every single wall in the place. (Even what looked like bodily fluids) then awoke to a massive colony of ants all over the wall in the kitchen area. Definitely needs abit more attention of cleaning to the older cabins will not be back.

Great location, very convenient for visiting other attractions in Canberra. There are some play facilities, though not very big, and they also have a restaurant. My room was clean, quite new, and well equipped. Suitable for a family stay.
Very nice. Restaurant is a great feature. Great pool area. Very friendly staff

We've stayed at a couple of different hotels in Canberra, however now that we've stayed at Alivio we'll never go anywhere else. Set in the most amazing location. Facilities were great and buffet breakfast awesome. So worth it!
Good

We had a wonderful stay! The reception staff were warm and welcoming, making us feel immediately at ease. Our cabin was great for our short visit, comfortable and well-appointed. The only minor issue was a dirty table at breakfast, likely due to the high number of guests at the buffet and the staff being understandably busy. Overall, the resort is beautifully spacious, and the range of facilities is excellent. Was so nice to wake up to the bush. Highly recommend!
I love it. This place very nice and clean. Staff is very friendly. Food cans beverages are good. Price friendly.

I recently stayed at Alivio Tourist Park Canberra and had a fantastic experience on both of my visits. The park itself is clean, well-maintained, and in a great location, but what really stood out was the exceptional service from Ahmad, the host/manager. He went above and beyond to make our stay comfortable, offering helpful advice, assistance whenever we needed it, and even providing us with discounts which made our trip even better value.

Ahmad’s warm and professional approach made us feel truly welcome, and it’s rare to come across such genuine hospitality. I would highly recommend Alivio Tourist Park to anyone visiting Canberra — not just for the great facilities, but also for the outstanding service. Thanks again to Ahmad and the team for making our stay so enjoyable!
Great facilities and good rates.

Always a great stop when I'm in Canberra. It's central to everything. Whether it's a caravan or a cabin, it's a lovely Park. Lots for Big and Little kids to do with a terrific recreational area including a great pool.
Excellent food in the restaurant also. I'll definitely enjoy staying here again soon. 😁

The facilities were ok some years ago but a lack of attention and maintenance let the whole place down. No cutlery in some rooms, no crockery in others. The lights would not work in the community kitchen. No on site maintenance and they had to be recalled specially on the weekend. They could do so much better.
So far so good, a little out of the city and away from the crowds but not that far from the restaurants and things to do.
Lovely and comfortable overnight stay

Great spot for a budget stay with family. Good location. Highly recommend the buffet breakfast - really well done and good value if you book the day before for a discount. The rooms are basic but clean and very functional and there's plenty for the kids to do. Will go back here again. Friendly birdies a plus!

We had a wonderful stay at the cabin. The place was spotless and well-equipped with everything we needed. The location was peaceful and surrounded by beautiful scenery. The staffs Charlie and Ahmed were friendly, responsive, and made us feel very welcome. Their warmth and genuine care made all the difference—- we truly felt valued as guests. The buffet breakfast was simply the best. A perfect start to the day. We will definitely return 👍
Good size room with all amenities.Has restaurant if you want meals.  Walking distance to bus to city area interchange and bus to major tourist spots. Had I issue at alivio but was sorted out quickly.

Not your usual caravan park at all. Excellent central location, wide variety of cabins, brilliant service from all staff I interacted with, and heaps of onsite services including food! Will definitely be coming back

Awesome location unreal amenities better than we where expecting. So close to the cbd but feels like your country camping with the mountains and thick bushland surrounding. If your planning a trip to canberra, we highly recommend the stay!
Great bar and food! Really good after the footy!
Humble and chill.
According to price isn’t as good as i expected room doesn’t have enough space air conditioning is soo bad

We had a great time at Alivio tourist park. The trip started poorly when we arrived and our room was double booked. Whilst a room upgrade wasn't available as everything was booked, we were well and truly compensated via park amenities and restaurant vouchers. When an upgrade became available, we were then transferred to this room and ended up having a great experience. I understand mistakes happen, but how the management team handled the issue was very impressive and we would be happy to return.

Good location, it has short bush walks around and the pool is fun.
Unfortunately the cabin wasn’t very clean. The floor was sticky, glasses in the cupboard were dirty and the bed covers were stained. It smelt like there had been smoking when we turned on the heat. All around a bit yuck.

Stayed 1 night. Cabin was basic however had everything we needed. It was clean and comfortable. Reception area including staff were excellent- easy and friendly checkin/out. Ate at the restaurant which was also good. Park was clean and in a lovely bush setting. BBQ facilities were great. Would definitely stay again if coming to Canberra

When I went here with my friends, there were a lot of strange people around here. There was an Indian man walking around and chasing children and then peeking through the windows with a flashlight. The buildings here are also quite old.
Cash not acceptable so I was moved on to another Park so unable to give a rating that would be fair 😔
Absolutely brilliant place to stay. Easy access for caravans with drive through sites. Location is so close to everywhere to get around Canberra. Park has great wildlife to.

For the price I guess I expected a little more. We stayed in a 3 bed villa, which was right in front of a massive hall, where sports teams gathered, they were boisterous and loud until midnight, both nights we were there. We have small children who did not sleep well due to the noise!
The food at the deck was not fantastic and we ate elsewhere the next night. The dryers weren’t working properly and we ended up having to run one load 4 times just to get it dry!!
Such a shame because the villa was lovely, beds were comfy, receptionist were great and super helpful. This place could be brilliant!

Room was dirty, bedding was stained and lounge cusions had blood stains on them. Second bedroom had an ant infestation.
Bathroom had long black hairs on the walls and only the heatlamp worked not the other globes. No dishwashing liquid under the sink to wash up, had to buy our own. Then the restaurant ordered our food, it was busy and our buzzer wasnt working ( which they said they knew about) the bartender then came up to our table 45 minutes later saying that our food was waiting for us for 15 minutes. So went and got the food and they had forgotten 3 dishes, no appology or compensation was offered. Really not happy at all
We stayed in a drive through Ensuite powered site.  The Ensuite was clean, however no soap supplied. Very convenient location, very close to CBD . Thankyou Alivio!

Have stayed here previously in a campervan at a drive-thru site. This time, booked a Woodland Cabin which is basic but sufficient for 1-2 people. Towels, linen, ironing board, iron, a/C, heater, spare blankets and some complimentary Toiletries provided (but of staying for more than a couple of days, I recommend bringing a spare roll of toilet paper with you so you don't get caught as a spare is not provided under the sink in the cabin). The cabin also has a good sized fridge and electric oven with cooktop, plus kettle and some basic tea, coffee, and sugar satchels. I recommend getting a barista coffee and buffet breakfast from the Verandah restaurant in the mornings (book ahead the day before and get a discount). Reception staff are super friendly, helpful, and efficient. Shared amenities (bathrooms, laundry, camp kitchen) are excellent too if staying in a campervan/caravan, but ensuite sites are also available which is awesome if ypu like your own private bathroom. Showers are clean and have excellent hot water and pressure. The Verandah restaurant is great of you want a cooked meal and a drink in the evening. Proximity to the city and surrounding suburbs is excellent, with bushland surrounds and lots of birdlife. I've stayed here for both business and pleasure and can definitely recommend if you are not a super-fussy person, enjoy your personal space, and don't wish to pay exorbitant hotel prices.

Great park, great amenities, but for caravan park user's it's very tight to position your van.
The top corner needs a major overall as its so tight of corner that you scrub out your tyres of caravan on the Square concrete edge curbing , especially with a van over 19ft. . Throughout the park the square edge curbing is a major hazard for tripping and car tyres.
Sorry but a major upgrade is required.
Cheers Kevin and Gayle Koop

Comfortable accommodation but the picture in the postings are a bit misleading. The living quarters are not as big as it seems in the pictures, they are quite crammed. Apart from that, the experience was pleasant.
Great place to stay, central to loads of things to visit, buffet breakfast was handy, huge holiday park,all amenities you'll need,cold beer, reasonable prices, friendly service

We absolutely loved our stay at Alivio Tourist Park – it was perfect for our family of six! From the moment we arrived, the staff were incredibly welcoming, helpful, and full of great suggestions for things to do around Canberra with the kids.

Our villa was spotless, modern, and super comfortable – it had everything we needed to feel right at home. We also really enjoyed our meals at The Verandah; both breakfast and dinner were delicious, and the kids were kept happy with the fun colouring activities while we relaxed.

There’s so much to do within the park itself – something for everyone, whether you want to unwind or keep the kids entertained. We couldn’t have asked for a better stay. Thank you, Alivio – we’ll definitely be back!

Lovely park for families.  There is lots of natural wildlife in the park as well (birds mostly).  Flat van sites and plenty of room.  There is a restaurant right near reception that is a godsend at the end of a long and busy day.  Nice facilities.

Great caravan park, lots of facilities great for families. 2 bedroom cabin, was good, a little tight but had everything we needed. Lots of communal spaces. Only downside not a lot of spare parking spaces if cabins have multiple cars you need to park at the front of the caravan park.

We usually stay here in winter. Staying in the 3 bedroom villa this time. We only had 1 mug for 8 people. We did ask for more but we never received them. We also had a nespresso machine and there were no pods for the machine, luckily I brought my own and there were no capsules for the washing machine.
The bunk room had melted chocolate chunks on the floor as well as on the curtains and marks here and there on doors and the walls. We wiped down some and cleaned as much of the chunks off the floor as I didn't want my 16month old to put in his mouth if he wandered into this room.
Lovely stay - 4 cabins next to the bush, group of 19 people…. Buffet breakfast was lovey and the Restaurant served good food. Customer service excellent

Great location but slow food service (understaffed?). The cabin had mould on the windowsills and was freezing cold even with air con going. Electric blankets would be a good addition for Canberra winters.

Wouldn’t recommend.
Late to check us in and when we arrived at the cabin, had to find the cleaning van to remove bags and dirty linen from porch. Room was not clean. Ant trail in main bedroom as sticky stuff on wall. The dust behind the beside tables was thick. No tea towels or cloths. Toilet seat was broken.
The ‘buffet’ breakfast is more like a camp breakfast. Ran out of cutlery numerous times. My daughter went to get a glass of juice but the containers were empty. Was told ‘that’s all there is.’ It had only been open half an hour. Disappointing experience
Amazing place for the family. Full of amenities, clean cabins and the staff members are friendly. Will definitely stay here again.

This place was so bad I stayed here and there was so many bed bugs the hair dryer was about 100 years old with tan lined it felt like we were sleeping on bricks the food was okay but the cabins were so old.

Stayed two nights in the 3 bedroom villa.
The villa itself was quite spacious. It had two large lounge suits and 8 seater table, and the living space still had plenty of room. Large balcony with a view of the bush. Bedrooms had great wardrobe space, beds were comfortable, and the pillows were nice.
The location is fantastic. You feel like you're in the bush, but only minutes from the city.
They have a restaurant on site, the meals were good, and buffet breakfast had a small selection but good hot food and the breakfast essentials (cereal, toast, coffee, etc).
The customer service was exceptional!

I've been a customer of Alivios for over 2 years now. Having to travel to Canberra for work, we always choose Alivios as they have amazing rooms, space, bbqs and service.

Almost everything about Alivios is amazing, perfect. The only issue I have is 'The Verendah', aka their restaurant.

The Verendah is a clean, welcoming, comfort style and is very nice to walk into and eat at. The staff are amazing, welcoming and informative. The issue with the restaurant is, it's getting a smaller menu but somehow still at a lower quality then when I started coming 2 years ago. The quality of the food is up and down and the variety is scarce. There isn't even a Parmi or a standard Burger. They have a bunch of fancy styled dishes that don't fit the vibe of the place. We had the Chicken Schnitty and the schnitzel looked premade and was bland and tasteless, the salad was dry and boring and the chips were unsalted. We also tried the Squid Ink Prawn Spaghetti, it was also bland, underseasened and the prawns tasted frozen, heck I'd even say tasteless and watery. Ontop of all that a normal 375ml JD can is $16.... ridiculous.

Overall Alivios has an OUTSTANDING room standards with all the essentials 👌  The rooms are a 5/5. The Restaurant needs to be reconsidered if thinking of eating there, unless they improve the quality and fix their 5main menu decision... no burger?! No Parmi?!?! Crazy.

Alivios Rooms, Outstanding
Restaurant 3/5, would only eat there if I was too tired to cook or go out.

Thank You Alivios for your amazing room quality. Will be staying here again 🫶🏻

Stayed in a Grevillea Cabin, Bed was comfortable, there are no electric blankets supplied, during the cold months, |Split system heater struggled to keep cabin warm, some days had a max temp of 4c.|Heater failed miserably when the night time temperatures dropped below 0c.|while we were there the heater stopped on several nights, about 2.00 am when the temperature dropped to -5.|The onsite restaurant called The Veranda  has gone downhill as well, it used to be a family friendly and reasonably priced.|But it is now overpriced for what you get, I ordered Crispy Pork Belly with aoli and Kiffler potatoes. The pork was  far from crispy, and it was served the upmarket way of a square of pork in the middle of the plate with a couple of dobs of aoli in the corners with two small roasted radishes,  and two inedible potatoes that were so hard you couldnt put a fork into them or even cut them with a steak knife. when I mentioned to staff I was told we will tell the chef.|Breakfast  Buffet was just luke warm , the only hot food was the toast you cook yourself. |All food was terrible very disappointed this stay.|Equipment in cabin was poor, Fire blanket was not packed properly, broom head was broken and broom unable to be used.
Stayed in the caravan part. Good, clean and close to city.

The cabin is neat and tidy with great facilities beside the rooms and towels. Good size fridge, microwave, sofa and stove. Parking is available right at the door which is great.
We stayed during one of the coldest nights and while there is only 1 aircon in the living area and not in the bedrooms, we appreciated that
Thank you
PHamily
Have been using Alivio for years. Rooms are spacious enough.

The staff are really friendly and kind.
Unfortunately the room we were in let this place down. The beds were itchy to sleep in, the shower floor was rotten and it felt like at any moment our feet would fall through the floor.
The dryers did not work after 2 x 45min drys. The washing machine spin cycle didn't spin well enough. After spending $48 on washing machines and dryers. I was not refunded any amount.
I had to go to a public laundromat to dry it all.

The place looks nice. But these little things were annoying. Also they charge $2 per towel if you are staying more then 1 night. No pool towels offered.
The pool shower is cold, not warm or hot.

We just enjoyed a family holiday and stayed at Alivio!

Cabins are all generally well appointed (though a bit dated) except for being equipped for the cold (as per other reviews) - which is disappointing given the colder Canberra climate (was actually a bit shocked by this)!

Our Cabin had tiles, with no central or floor heating. Our cabin was also older with one small split cycle in the living room which didn't heat the two rear bedrooms well. Our daughters room without any additional heat was 13 degrees at 7pm.
We had to go to reception and ask for a heater or more blankets - in which they did bring us a heater (am glad as reading other reviews was concerned is have to do a Kmart dash)!

Would really benefit from additional blankets in the rooms (there was one extra per room) an additional heater like an oil heater in the cupboards. Especially if families are staying with small children.

Our shower also didn't drain very well and we water logged a few towels trying to prevent the entire bathroom floor from becoming wet! Shame as you could see it has been renovated.

The newer cabins at the front have much bigger air conditioning units which seems to heat the cabins much better, which may be a better option if you're worried about the cold and have smaller children.

Otherwise, the facilities are amazing and can see a lot of care and upgrades going into the parks amenities. Great place for kids in warmer weather with basketball court, playground, tennis court, splash pad and bbq area!

The location is also so central so was beneficial to get around to do all the attractions and get to breakfast / dinner spots.
Recently stayed not recommended in winter due to poor quality of their heating system, totally waste of money .
I went on school camp,this was amazing,however,we need more towels and make egg less gooey,apart from that all good
在这里住过好几次了，小木屋干净整洁，温馨舒适，以后还会再来的。
Pretty good little caravan park

Well, we’re back again at Alivio Tourist Park — this time over the ANZAC long weekend! Our first visit was with the caravan, but this time we opted for a cabin as we were travelling with other family members. It was a pleasant stay overall.

The park had some great activities for the kids, including a scavenger hunt and late afternoon face painting, which they absolutely loved. We also took one of the park's e-bikes for a spin around Canberra — a fun experience, though the hire cost was a bit steep in my opinion.

The kids had a blast throughout the weekend, which is always a win. There are still a few areas that could use some attention — particularly the kids’ games room, which hasn’t changed in years and has some aging equipment. We also noticed groups of kids riding scooters unsupervised at night, doing burnouts and causing a bit of noise and smell, which was a bit disruptive.

The cabin, while basic, was clean and comfortable and still holding up well considering their age.

All in all, it was a great weekend, and we’ll happily consider coming back again for another family stay.
Very well organised park
Well set out for caravans, good pool and bar
Pricier than others but convenient for us.

The best caravan park in Canberra. Walking tracks to Black Mountain and Bruce Reserve right from the front gate, restaurant, bar, pools, well maintained facilities, close to Canberra city. What more could anyone ask for? I note a few whingers with petty First World complaints, but these should be ignored.

Immaculate, quiet, and the staff are kind and helpful. The cockatoos were the loudest thing in the park. Pull through spaces are paved and level for eadt camping. Be sure to tell Eddie at the reception your favorite dad joke - he collects them!

We stayed for a week in a drive through caravan site. The ammenities were always clean. The sites were large. The park was close to all Canberras attractions We had a great stay and to top it off we dined at the park restaurant and had an excellent meal which topped the week off perfectly
Great price, Service and facilities. Everything was clean and worked well.
Clean and comfortable. The restaurant and bar was great value
Good and fast service. We didnt get a dishwashing liquid, but we only stayed for 2 nights, so no big deal. Lots of amenities that kids can enjoy!

This is a great tourist park, will definitely stay again. Not over crowded, drive through caravan parks and en-suite suites, great amenities for kids and families and a 3 hour happy hour. Cant go wrong!
Cabins are very basic and dated however they were very clean
Not bad
Easy checkin. Great location to all Canberra attractions. Fantastic place to stay with children.
Bardzo miła obsługa! Polecam bardzo pomocnego pana z recepcji 🙂
Good family accommodation with good running trail

This place is the best, prettiest, well designed caravan park I have ever been to.
We stayed in one of the older cabins but that doesn't matter. The place is well cleaned and nicely presented.
The best thing about Alivio is the outdoor facilities, playgrounds, sports facilities (tennis, bball, vball,ping pong, foosball), pool, many outdoor dining areas that are scattered in the tourist park - decorated with beautiful fairy lights. They also have an edible garden with lovely herbs growing in them which we did harvest for dinner.

The park has everything you need including coin laundry and a restaurant onsite which serves all meals and coffees.

I told the reception about the things (wobbly table, door hinge missing screws, outdoor towel hanger falling off) in the cabin which needed fixing and by the time we came back in the evening, they were all fixed.

The cabin has a sizeable fridge, coffee tea sugar, kettle, toaster, oven, microwave, 2 sets of pots, a saucepan, a stainless steel bowl, a collander, cutleries, glassware, crockeries, cooking utensils, a very blunt chef's knife, a peeler, and chopping board,.

Located so close to the city, but has a total bush vibe, even saw a couple of kangaroos at dusk foraging. I had the best time here with the family! Highly recommend!

Second review as the first was not published. They seem to only print the good one. Ablutions are absolutely filthy. Only got cleaned twice when I complained in 7 days. $75 dollars a night for a caravan site. Overpriced considering the facilities are in such a bad state. Let's see if this is published.

We have stayed at the park three times now and our last experience this weekend was not pleasant. Our cabin was very grotty there was black hair and mould in the shower, I moved the side table next to the bed to plug chargers in and there was thick layers of dust and just to top it all off there was old blood on one of the bunk bed sheets, so my kids had to share a bed because no way in hell were they sleeping in someone else’s blood. (See pictures attached). When I told the lady at reception all she said was “oh sorry” you should of called the on call manager… sorry love but at 10PM at night I’m not sure they would be impressed on doing a deep clean of a cabin and have my kids awake till midnight while they cleaned. Such a shame but I don’t think we will be back again.

Really good place. Staff are friendly. Rooms clean and alot to offer guests. One thing that is the main let down is the on site restaurant. On paper the food looks good. In reality it was overpriced, low quality food. Night 1 I got the lamb shanks, it was disappointing. Tasted liked it was cooked a few days ago, it was tough and not good at all. The cous cous that accompanied it was delicious.  My wife got the fish and chips. It looked and tasted like it was out of a box. These two meals and two kids meals cost over $100. Night 2 I ordered a pizza to take back to my cabin I then see the pizza maker rip open a pre made pizza base out of a packet. Now if I'm paying $25 for this it would be nice if the pizza dough was made fresh. So I would recommend staying here but not eating here
Pools and splash parks are always a winner and the cabins were well appointed and good value. Dining amenities on site and convenient to get to everywhere. Very happy with the place.

Great location in our amazing capital city of Canberra.
Alivio has excellent family facility’s & a restaurant.

We have been at the park for a week and the ablution/amenities block has only been cleaned once. I telephoned reception after 3 days of no action and reported that the block was filthy with paper on the floors, feacies of the rear of the toilet seats. The block was then cleaned the next day. I then went to the office on the 7th day and reported that the block was again filthy and had not been cleaned since the last time. I was told it "should" get cleaned every couple of days, even though the signage on the door states is done twice a day. There are 31 caravan sites, some with ensuites, available everyday and most have been occupied during our stay. The quantity of people and the cleaning regime is simply not up to anyones standards, especially when I'm paying over $74 per night. The toilet seats are loose, the floors are dirty, the showers have a build up of scum on the walls and floor, this is the dirtiest amenities block I have ever had the misfortune of using. I will never stay here again and will definitely not promote this park as a place to stay.

We enjoyed our stay here. We were in the newer cabins, they are more expensive however they are modern and fairly new which was nice.

Park has good parking and facilities like pool, spag play and playground.

The biggest let down is the restaurant onsite. It’s a sham, sells you a buffet breakfast for a set price but only provided half of the things listed. When I questioned them the staff just said “we don’t have it sorry”. I have reviewed the restaurant separately in more detail.

Set amongst a nature reserve, it was good to head back here after a day in busy Canberra.
My favourite place to stay every time I visit Canberra

Hats off to Josh at The Verandah Restaurant! After a hiccup during a birthday celebration, he handled it swiftly, professionally, and with genuine empathy. He went above and beyond to make things right—plus a birthday drink was a thoughtful touch. Thanks, Josh, for your kindness and top-notch service!

The location is beautiful, quite, based in nature, yet 5 minutes to Canberra Central. Beds were comfy and soft, swimming pool, spa, half court, volleyball court, tennis court, playgrounds. The room was lacking basic resources, air conditioner remote, TV working remote ( we had 2 tvs), coffee pods.
Neat, tidy, and spacious rooms, lots to do at the park and walking distance to GIO stadium.
Absolutely superb park.

Great location is the only good thing about this park.
Cabin - smelt like sewerage.
Dinner - can get more toppings on a supermarket frozen pizza.
Breakfast - quality does not match exorbitant price.
Exactly what you expect
Bar staff, although super friendly, were hopeless, 3 staff, 3 customers with easy orders, and it still took 10 minutes

We stay here every year for 2 nights. It's basketball weekend. We have done this 4 years in a row. We won't be back for a 5th. Boronia villas at the front not cheap so I expect clean. The bathroom was filthy, apple juice container on the floor, chop stick and spoon the deck, the kitchen didn't have anything replenished (no nespresso pods, sugar, no dishwasher  liquid or tea towel). Went to reception to tell them. No apology or offer to re check just handed me the things. I had 2 cabins and both were the same.
Then the bar special cocktail of the day , espresso martini, no kahlua. Ok just swap it out with baileys. Nope couldn't do that either. Than why put it on your specials boards.
Excellent setup, great for families, clean and lots of space.

Nice, big, well set-out, well located. Ensuite was a good little bonus, however the let-down was an unnecessary lack of attention to detail. Never before has any holiday accomodation failed to supply basic welcoming items such as complimentary soap, shampoo and such. Scummy. It means as soon as tired guests arrive it's a mad dash in an unknown city to find a shop for necessities. Minor details make all the difference re rebooking or recommending.

I wasn’t expecting this to be a holiday park, but the location was perfect. However, as soon as we checked into our cabin, we found it to be average in cleanliness, with spider webs everywhere and German cockroaches all over. The bed sheets and blankets were also dirty.

On the positive side, the staff were very friendly and helpful, always greeting us with a big smile. The restaurant was excellent, offering affordable prices and five-star service.

I understand that this park is in the bush, but they really need to focus more on cleaning and pest control. Unfortunately, I’m not sure if I would return to this park again.

The grounds, facilities, restaurant and bar are excellent but I would only stay again if I paid the much higher price for the new cabins.
We went 'mid price' and were in one of the older cabins and it was actually quite awful. So tiny, bathroom was unpleasant, walls do like cardboard, AC barely made a difference, no outside area, beds below standard.
These old cabins have got to go or else make it very clear in advertising that they are BASIC.
Made the overall stay disappointing.

The park itself is nice. We are currently staying at a site with ensuit amenities, which is wonderful. Amenities are nice and clean. The park lacks of green though. There is lot of dirt/soil but no grass. So our kids are dirty all the time when they run around outside the camper. The pool area is very nice, pool itself is nice and clean. The restaurant area looks very nice, there are even toys for the kids to play at. WiFi is not good.
Don’t book the breakfast buffet though. There is not enough staff to manage the workload. Everywhere you can see tables stacked with dirty dishes, nobody cleans the tables. Coffee is not included in the high price, there are no fresh fruits at the buffet (just canned ones- like in a cafeteria in a youth hostel), there was no butter left after a while, nothing but honey and jam to put on your toast (no cheese or anything). They ran out of mugs half way through breakfast time. No muesli/granola, just two types of basic cereals. No kids cereals. Kids from 0-5 pay 5$, which is not much, but a baby (especially my baby, which is still nursing) will not eat anything from the buffet. The buffet lacks healthy food at all. Would not book this again.

Very disappointing.

Definitely would not recommend for Caravan or Motorhome travellers, especially at $75 per night.

The site was covered in sand that had washed down from the site above and had obviously been that way for some time as there were shoe prints in the sand that couldn’t be removed with a broom.

I complained and they sent over the pool life guard with a broom who said he couldn’t do it as he was on pool duty. I swept it myself.

Then early on the final morning we were locked out of the amenities (as was everyone else leaving that day) when the access key failed to work !

Tired and in need of a revamp. Nice buffet breakfast in the morning at the dining area. Went to dine at night  verified that the restaurant was open, parked our car and then ordered drinks before perusing the menu. went back to order our meals and was told the chef had closed the kitchen and all that was available was pizza. Pathetic
Really helpful staff, great amenities on site, very good for children. Great range of rooms and flexible with bookings.
The facilities are superbly kept...Eddie was magnificent along Lauren i think her name was..really helpful definitely my go to place in Canberra.
Great place 😀
很棒。小木屋有廚房，瓦斯爐，烤箱，微波爐一應具全。小孩很喜歡泳池。

Stayed during January for a week while at a work course, most of the staff were amazing and so accomodating. Couldn’t thank Eddie and Josh enough for going above and beyond with their hospitality and making our week as amazing as they did. The facilities were great, breakfast and coffee were fantastic. 100% will be returning.
The place is very big and clean.

The Cabin was great and the kids lived the swimming pool. Had a small issue with our meal at the restaurant but the lovely staff compensated us for. Overall we were happy with the service and the cabin we stayed in. We even saw some of the furry locals.

Friendly staff. Good location. Excellent MTB trials.
Good restaurant.

Friendly place. Good for families. Good MTB tracks. |Fun pool, tennis court and good restaurant.|Eddie the restaurant manager was very helpful. |Nice choice of accommodation. Clean facilities. Nice view of the bush. |We had an extended family gathering.

I visited  others staying in the park and I ate at the Restaurant with them
8 of us. Food and drinks were good but a little expensive.

Beautiful new cabin. Very clean. Comfortable bedding.
Blind could have been put up in kitchen window; but apart from that our stay was very lovely.
Restaurant staff were very friendly. Kids loved the pool and play areas.

A beautiful place for a family trip! The rooms were great for the size, however ours did have a urine scent to it and there was some mould throughout. The staff service was great upon arrive.

Would NOT recommend dining at ‘The Verandah’ inside dining, the food was simple and lacked flavour.

The only flaw when going with younger kids is the ‘hillness’ of the park, otherwise ample activities available.

Upgraded our room which was an easy process with Soviet at reception. Soviet was absolutely fantastic and nothing was too much trouble.
Pool area is great but there was a large bowel movement on the floor of the attached women's bathroom, it had obviously been there for awhile.
Unsure If it's related to the hygiene standard of the park but 2/3 families we travelled with had violent gastro within a day of leaving the park. I did email reception to notify  of infectious illness but havnt received a response.
Restaurant is very hit and miss. They do lamb shanks and parmi very well, but Pork belly and salt and pepper squid were very over cooked.
There is an African manager who does the afternoon shift, he was absolutely wonderful to deal with and very cheerful! Aside from him and Soviet, the rest of the staff we encountered looked like they were being held hostage lol.

Kids activities were fantastic! The BBQ and seating facilities were wonderful. Kids absolutely loved the face painting each afternoon.
The newer cabins are wonderful.

We would be open to staying again.

Really lovely caravan park, plenty of activities for kids, clean grounds and pool area, well set out. Unfortunately did not see any vacated ensuite sites be cleaned during our week stay. Nice layout of camp kitchen but no cleaning utilities available to clean up after use

Stayed for 3 nights in Bluegum villa. Location fantastic. Poor layout of access paths to cabins as path lead direct to back of car parking with no way around cars so our car got a large scratch from a kids bike handle as they squeeze between the vehicles. Yes, this could happen anywhere but attention to detail clearly lacking in layout. Totally agree with other reviews that house keeping is a bit of a joke! Hair all in bathroom, on walls, on drains and floors. Toilet was terrible! See pic. No way that toilet has been cleaned for atleast a month! Bathroom in villa #14 leaks water into ajoining bedroom and even had water coming up inbetween floor tiles in walkway outside of bathroom. Clearly a terrible waterproofing job done but this is their problem not mine so we weren't bothered about it but thought we would note it for the owners sake.
