Park seems to have all amenities but we didnt use them. We would consider coming back but you really need to improve the cleanliness of the accomodation.

We stayed here in a cabin December/January, Eddie is the man he was so accomodating, any questions we had he was more than happy to answer them he is a great asset to Alivio..we highly recommend Alivio if you need somewhere to stay while in Canberra, the kids loved the pool area! Only thing I could suggest is some extra things on the buffet breakfast menu- maybe pancakes for kids but other than that we were very happy with our stay and have already booked for next year!

Great for being close to hospital which is what we required but also for shops as well.

Just stayed again and this time in a cabin.  Had all we needed and clean.
We needed to change our length of stay and had an excellent outcome.  We will be returning in a few  months again.  Highly recommend.
Great facilities, lots of different accommodation options, my only criticism is I wish there was public transport nearby or some sort of shuttle to the city.

Great service
Clean amenities
1st class accommodation

Will not be staying again. What a let down. Alivio has great facilities but unfortunately the staff really let this place down. Firstly as someone with a child with a disability, there was not one designated disability parking spot in the whole of the complex. Food is sub average to say the least. When we entered our cabin, we found dirty sheets. Food still under the bed and some cutlery not washed up. 1 star for housekeeping at the best. We had the buffet breakfast option on our 2nd morning and it just went down hill from there. The staff were not helpful and gentleman running the morning service needs to learn what customer service is and not raise his voice in a disgusted manner when we asked if they had decaf coffee. Food was tasteless and some items very difficult to get to without stretching across the from food items. The next day we ordered an afternoon snack of salt and pepper squid and some fried chicken wings.
The salt and pepper squid came out on a bed of chillies with the seeds all over the food. This was not advertised to come with chillies so beware if you have an allergy. We had to send the chickens wings back as they were brought to us stone cold and looked like they had been boiled not fried. After taking them back the bartender apologised and told us the must have forgot to microwave them as this is their normal practice. Great location but unfortunately the cabins need a lot of updating and fresh staff who actually want to be there would also help.

Beds are rock hard.
Cleaners sitting down in rooms eating food while supposed to be daily service.
Don't eat in restaurant, they offer fried chicken wings that the staff advise they are just microwaved. Really rude service from staff.

We've been staying at this place for 5+ years.. normally booking 2 or 3 villas

The cleanliness has gone down the drain

Check in times of 2pm have us waiting 2-3 hours longer than advertised

Kitchens have very limited items to cook with

The park is clearly now motivated by Money over anything else... its a business I get it, I have my own business aswell, but now wanting to charge almost $800 a night for a villa which is less than $300 normally is absolutely taking the Piss... and u should be ashamed of yourself

WE WONT BE BACK!
The bloke named Eddie was awsome will be back in feb

Booked villa on behalf of my daughter’s family of three visiting from Adelaide for three nights.  They were most impressed by the comfortable and roomy accommodation and appreciated the pool and bike paths.

Can not say a bad thing about this tourist park. Location is perfect and staff you can’t get better service anywhere else.
They are extremely helpful and supportive for the car enthusiasts for Summernats.
We traveled with friends with the pink beach biggie  looking forward to the next Summernats
Eddie what can I say.. we have stayed at the park before and Eddie made the stay even better with his personality he is a keeper.

Unfortunately the room wasnt professionally cleaned before the checkin, there was a bird inside the cabin that freaked everyone out, and it was a hassle to get rid of it,
The management didnt collaborate when refund requested and they were not even communicating about all raised issues at all, lack of responsibility and accountability
Convenient location, good facilities, reasonable price.
Great clean friendly

From the moment we checked in to the time we checked out we had a wonderful stay! We had a drive through caravan site with ensuite - the facilities were clean and well cared for. Particularly Eddy on the front desk was friendly and helpful and really went the extra mile! Thanks Eddy!  We highly recommend this caravan park and we will be back.
Great place to stay. Very clean. Reasonable pricing. Great food available from the on site restaurants. Highly recommended.
The park is great for kids with the pools and activities. However, the room is a bit small - need the bench space to store and prepare formula milk for the toddler.
Very average, all show no go. Price per night greatly exceeds value. Staff were rude and inexperienced. In particular Bar staff, repeatly lying about advertised drinks menu?

In general, we liked our stay here, check in and out process was easy and bed was comfy. The room was equipped with all necessary equipment but it feels a bit outdated. I could’ve stayed in a 4 star hotel just by paying a few extra dollars. The main reason we wanna stay here is because we wanna experience living in a cabin..

Anyway, we are happy with everything here so far, will reconsider staying here again in the future

We are staying in a Grevillea Cabin. It’s very dated and compact but most importantly it’s very clean and quiet. (Better than the Ridgeview Cabins - they are really tiny).
I can’t say the people working on reception are friendly or welcoming but they get the job done. The cabin fridge wasn’t working upon arrival but it was replaced within 15 minutes of us informing them. The cabin didn’t have a dish sponge or tea towel. When we requested these items reception replied with a sigh before providing us with the items.
The restaurant and pool area is great. Its the park’s best asset. The food is also good.
Annoyingly the EV chargers are only available to the guests staying in the Boronia Villas.
Having said all this, we are returning patrons as the place serves us well for the purpose of our visits to Canberra. And I would actually recommend this place to family and friends.

Highly recommended.
We are regular visitors to Alivio, location being a significant factor: they are located 20 minutes drive from family who we are there to see.
Alivio offers a great facility ranging from drive-through van sites to various grades of cabins.
Pool and playground equipment are well-designed and cater for a variety of ages.
The restaurant offers good meals with plenty of indoor or outdoor seating. Best to check restaurant opening times.

On arrival was given the wrong room, but wasn't notified until after we had unpacked our bags. Was then moved to a Wattle Glen Cabin. The bed was very uncomfortable, and not much sleep was had. Very small bathroom. Shower curtain didn't keep water from getting on bathroom floor. Paid $250 in off-peak season for one night. Felt it was overpriced compared to a motel room with kitchenette.
Toilet is blocked which we didn't expect......but the location is not bad.

Unreal oasis in the heart of the bush Capitol. Central to everything without even needing to leave. Top tier pool facility with multiple parks, tennis court, basketball, barbecue areas, indoor areas and a full service bar and impressive restaurant. Gourmet buffet breakfast and really good coffee shop. The downside may be the price but if you factor in the activities for the family and their kid friendly management it's money we'll spent. Really good value overall!

Good location, nice pool area. Grevillea cabin underwhelming.

Some of the newer (much more expensive) Villas look nice and may be well-equipped, but we were a little disappointed with our Grevillea cabin. It is advertised as sleeping five people, but there were not even five full sets of crockery or cutlery. It was generally a little run down and neglected - for example, the kitchen tidy was missing its lid, and there was a broom but no dustpan.

The caravan area looks practical (drive-through is a plus - no reversing required), but is rather barren so if you want to sit outside you'll just be looking at other vans.

The location is convenient to the city and main attractions, with a nature reserve next door, and a nice view of Telstra Tower. Kangaroos can be found over the road.

The bar and pool area are modern and attractive. Staff friendly enough.

I liked the place and it had a great pool. Although the cabins have no anti spider, cockroach and I find them freaky. Everywhere I look I see a spider or cockroach and I would honestly pay a bit more to get a room without a spider on everything and cockroach in every corner. I went to bed and I found an old blood stain on my blanket. I do NOT recommend this place to anyone!
Very comfortable excellent staff great location

We arrived at our room and found hair from the previous tenant everywhere and it looked like someone had emptied the toaster tray on the floor. We called reception and they sent someone right away to fix it up, which is great, but it's strange that it was like that in the first place.
The beds were fine, the kitchenette was good, but the cabin was a little run down. Some of the curtains were torn in the bedroom, the toilet brush was unusable, there was no scrubbing brush to wash dishes, tv remotes were missing batteries, and the guest wifi was extremely patchy.
The pool was the highlight for us. We spent a few hours swimming and our 3 year old had a great time with the sprinkler things. The rest of the facilities looked good but we didn't use any as we weren't there for long.
We were lucky enough to have a cabin looking out over the bush, and loads of kangaroos hung out there for a few hours in the afternoon. You could easily see them from the little balcony and the kitchen window.
Not a bad experience at all, but nothing outstanding. I'd recommend coming here if you have an active family who isn't going to spend too much time in the cabins
Ahmed provides exceptional customer service, always attentive, professional, and efficient. He goes above and beyond to ensure every customer leaves satisfied, making him a standout in his role.
Fabulous resort for a family holiday - about to go for third trip and will be rebooking for next year. Pool awesome and dinner lovely.

Stayed for 2 nights in a caravan site. Its a nice park but I think it's way over priced for what you get...$105 per night for a caravan site for 2 adults and 1 small child. We gave stayed at similar parks with on par facilities for nearly half the price.
The cleanliness of the toilets ans bathroom need attention .
The front reception staff are absolutely lovely and very helpful.

this is a dump. we have found bed bugs in 5+ of your cabins. you must be not paying the cleaners cause they do not do their job. its ridiculous!! i will not be coming back here again what a ridiculous place. and dont even get me started on the staffs behaviour. its to bad to write in a review. please shut done.
Stayed numerous times at Boronia. Always need to ask for coffee pods.
hard to find the room number

Yep found musty crusty nerds in my bed, but it’s ok! The toenails were fresh! Also was very suspicious white liquid on my curtains, tasted a bit salty….played a game trying to get the crusty nerds into a hole i found in the wall. The bed bugs were loving it, cheering me on by biting my 16 times. Good job canberra, really living up to the australian name.

Great resort nestled in the bush against the slopes of Bruce Ridge. Considering the setting, it's quite a surprise to discover how close to the city it is. Perfect distance for an electric bike ride to Lake Burley Griffin, and there is actually a self-service eBike hire station right there.  Way cheaper than the orange scooter menace. Recommended.
Great spot, plenty to do at the park and even somewhere for breakfast and dinner.

We rented a cabin for the night. The cabin was large and well furnished with a great outdoor deck. Unfortunately there was a small inefficient aircon and one fan in the lounge area. The bedrooms did not have any cooling at all and were very uncomfortable on a hot night.
There was also quite a bit of party noise until well after 2am.
The location is fantastic for a visit to Canberra.

Spoke to Duty manager Eddie, about an issue, and he was so helpful, and charming, he certainly was great with customer service.  the park itself is great, and has always been a favorite place of ours to holiday from Adelaide

Great location.  We rented a cabin.  Had aircon, 2 br, one with double bed, one with 4 bunks.  Rooms were very small, but bed was comfortable.  Shower curtain was a bit mouldy, but apart from that all good!  The food onsite was food for dinner, and just watch the cheeky birds who will dive bomb you for your food if you eat outside!

Love this park. There is something for all ages.  The pool is amazing.  Easy drive through van sites.  The ensuites are clean and spacious, although you need to bring all your own supplies, eg, shampoo and soap. We will definitely be staying again.
We could not get internet service in the park.

We spent one night in one of the older style cabins , it was affordable at the time however looking back I wish I had of spent the extra money and got one of the new ones. The cabin is extremely dated and just felt grubby. the bedside table to the main bed was crawling with larvae which I presume belonged to the blow flys buzzing round in the room on arrival.
I spoke to reception and showed them a video of what I found, who simply handed me a bottle of spray and wipe and a cloth so I could go and clean it.
Despite knowing of our intended arrival the air con had not been turned on ( which we have found most other cabin parks we have stayed at do in preparation for guests ) and the room was like a sauna.
It’s a pitty because the venue and communal facilities looked amazing. So if you are booking there either take your caravan or get one of the much newer style cabins

The rooms were very pricey for 1 week but the up side of staying here is the pool and amenities that come with the tourist park. The only problem was the bathroom floor was slowly rotting away and it felt like you would fall through it so easily and the cabins weren’t the biggest for the price you paid.
The restaurant apart of the tourist park was less then ok the food was never restocked for the breakfast buffet and the pizzas for dinner was as thin as paper anyway my over all view is stay here but Ho for the higher price ones
Great place for families, pool and sport facilities are great. Good location. We got the cheapest cabin type so wasn't super high quality but it  was comfortable and had everything we needed.
Great staff, well maintained park. Fantastic facilities

`"I recently stayed at Alivio Tourist Park, hoping for a relaxing experience, but unfortunately, my visit was anything but that. The customer service was appalling, and it was largely due to the unbelievably rude behavior of the manager on duty. From the moment I arrived, the attitude was dismissive, making it clear that guests’ needs were not a priority. When I raised a simple question about my reservation, I was met with hostility and condescension that left me stunned.

The management’s behavior was unprofessional and absolutely unacceptable, creating a tense and uncomfortable atmosphere for the duration of my stay. The stay was okay, but our cabin had a weird smell in the bathroom, some of the curtains were torn and were stained with food. The basic cabin's are overpriced by $50-70 a night for the outdated condition they are in.

Very nice and affordable place to stay. The resort location is the highlight and is near to all the major tourist attractions. Lots of places for the kids to play. Swimming pool is highlight of the stay ,kids throughly enjoyed the pool.
The restaurant inside the resort is very good and has lots of tasty foods.

The cabin we stayed was very good and spacious. Only draw back is the bathroom was not cleaned at all. Had a very bad smell. After our late night check in I ended up cleaning the toilet and bathroom floor.

Guest can smoke outside the cabin unfortunately our cabin was near the main area and some guests Wer smoking outside our cabin car park so the smoke was inside our bedroom too. That make me very uncomfortable as I have asthma

The kind folk from Alivio very kindly host us each month after our working bees in the surrounding bushland.  Providing us with a welcome cool drink and some very tasty pizza.  Thank you so much!  Friends of Dryandra Woodland

We didn’t stay in a room but were staying in our camper. The price for the site- with only 2 people  - was very high compared to normal caravan parks. All you get is access to the amenities. They were not clean and no one cleaned them or attended to them in the 3 nights we stayed there. Then there was the alleged great breakfast that we had to book the night before. Limited choice buffet where half of the items had run out ( such as the eggs) and not replaced. Had to pay extra for a decent coffee. Didn’t even have real butter for the toast- some vegan rubbish. No staff on hand either. Eat somewhere else!

Amazing
Great location nice private tourist Park
Restaurant on site
Cabins are roomy and clean
Pool area is a must
We've already rebooked for the January school holidays

There is plenty of room for two.  Neat and tidy outfitting,  comfortable beds, good a/c.  Lovely staff, so helpful and prompt with minor maintenance issues.  Our stay in October was  happy and relaxed.   Trevor Sargent Busselton WA

Grat tourist park with own bistro and bar. Clean grounds and cabins.
Would stay again.

Beautiful big park.  Drive through sites.  Modern clean amenities.  Restaurant on site which also has buffet breakfast.  Pool water park half court basketball court volley ball court tennis court playground. One of the best we've stayed at

.your response is sad ...just sad

Having seen this place on the show " what's up down under"
Then reading even recent reviews on line about how filthy the place is ...I'll gladly spend my money elsewhere

Hope others are smart enough to search reviews 1st
We stayed over AFL Grand Final weekend & apart from being almost mowed down by one of the many speeding drivers in the park it was great! Plenty of facilities & well maintained/clean.
Awesome experience, family friendly as the kids enjoyed the parks and pool. Cabins were small but enough for our family of 5(Young kids) beautiful service and great location.

We have been coming her for 3 years always over the October long weekend. First year couldn't fault anything from cabins to the restaurant and service. The past 2 years on the other hand and this is related to the restaurant and customer service it has gone down the drain the restaurant staff particularly the manager is rude and arrogant the food is overpriced and poor quality. The so called kids gaming room is run down dirty smells and the games tables are old and falling appart and missing the items required to play said games. This will be our last time staying here.
Good facilities, dirty cabins. The staff have apparently never heard of a mop or vacuum cleaner. Everything below knee height was stained and filthy, including the chairs and sofa. Never again.

We have stayed here multiple times over the years in the Park Villas and usually no issues. But this time it just wasn’t up there. The bed was the worst - so hard and woke up both mornings with issues. The villa stunk of take out food. The pillows flat and not worth lieing on.  Dust on the bed side tables.|The shower was the best though! |I think it is a good park for the younger kids, lots to do with playground, tennis court and pool. |Now with our kids older, I think this will be our last stay there.|It was a quiet park though
I recommend this hotel so fabulous. We loved to book here everytime we go to Canberra not far from the city...
Beautiful wildlife and everything you could need for a holiday. Cleaners need to look under the bed, there were empty beer bottles.

Just checked in, feel like crying! Absolutely old and disgusting cabin. Something out of the 70s|We are here for the Transplant games this week. All are immune compromised and we are in dirty, grungy old  cabin. For over $1300 for a week it is absolutely 🤮 disgusting |Only clicked one circle as no option for less.

The cabin has the amenities needed to make your stay really comfortable. The place has facilities that family can enjoy like pool, spa and playground. Location is good especially for people who loves nature.
In general, the place was clean but not enough as my children accidentally found bottles of beer underneath their bed. It is sorrounded by nature but would have been nice to use surface spray once in a while as we found a cochroach inside our room.

filthy cabins, not ready upon late arrival, changed cabins after discussion with staff and offered a free breakfast as compensation.  original cabins were trashed by previous guests and 'fixed' "signed off by cleaners' all we got was excuses. when in the new 'cleaned cabins', our son and partner had a dirty nappy in the rubbish bin, a cot was requested for their infant child, paid $40 (4 night stay). staff dropped cot off and went to leave, when asked if he could erect the cot, NO IT IS NOT MY JOB. No mattress with cot, again NOT MY JOB and WE DON'T SULLPY A COT MATTRESS. spoke to management again, Yes they do come with a mattress and one would be delivered soon.  Not mattress, only a blanket delivered and folded to resemble a mattress.  following day, friends arrived in another cabin, dishes were filthy (salt/pepper and grease on most plates.  Again all cabins were 'signed off'  as 'cleaned. we were there for 4 nights, expected to do dishes and clean after us.  kitchen had no detergent to wash dishes.  rubbish bins in the cabins were small with only 1 extra bag to use. |severe lack of outside rubbish bins outside (industrial bins scattered here and there)|When paying $1000+ for deluxe accommodation for a 4 night stay, left with on a sour note.||would we be back,  NO

Great place to stay.
Lots of things for kids to do. There is tennis court, basketball court, playground, water play, swimming pool, spa. A few bbq areas with tables and seating around.
The park is very clean and tidy. It was also quiet and peaceful.
We had a two bed cabin with kitchen, dinner table, 2 x TV's, couch and bathroom. Kitchen was great with full size fridge, freezer, oven, electric cooktop and microwave.
Plenty of hot water.
Guess wifi works allright.
Comfy beds.
Cabin was a great size.
Friendly staff.
Spa could be hotter, it was a bit cold.
The verahdah restraunt does a good dinner service and breakfast service. But Sat night dinner time was very busy.
It is located a short drive from some supermarkets and main roads. So easy to get around from here.
Laundry was $6 to wash and $6 for the dryer.
We enjoyed our stay here and would love to stay here again.

Stayed 4 nites. Staff happily changed us from the first cabin into a much better one. First one had very soft worn out mattresses. The upgrade at no extra cost was a beautiful new cabin, very comfortable. Lots of wildlife around, kangaroos  Rosella's, cockatoos.
Great stay.

Stayed in the Grevillea Cabins, and on arrival it definitely hadn’t been swept or mopped, front deck had beer bottle caps over it, we called reception manager came and had cleaners come and mop and clean the toilet again. BUT it wasn’t just general cleaning the entire cabin was filthy. |Black mould on silicon on all sinks, |Dust on top of fridge |Filthy walls, furniture and carpets |Mattresses are yellow. |Found cruisers bottles under the single bed that had been there that long they had a build up of dust on them. |Paid $720 for 3 nights I could see from photos it was outdated but I didn’t mind, however what I do mind is being in a filthy cabin. |Compensation was ONE free breakfast.

Unknown to my knowledge on arrival after booking, and paying! You then have pay extra for cot arrival, which they don't even set up for you, just dumped onto the sofa and then I had to ask for extra blanket for the cot.
My bed was horrible, was like sleeping plastic hospital bed. Worst sleep ever!

Stayed here last year on the same weekend and had a good experience and decided to stay again. This year the experience was not so good. Arrived to check in around 12pm, we were told to try again later as the cabin was not clean. It was after 4pm when we could check in and the cleaner was still in the  cabin when we tried to go inside. We then tried to book in for dinner at the restaurant and was told it was fully booked out. All of the camp kitchens had also been booked out or were full. I feel if you can provide accommodation for this many people you should have the facilities to service them. Obviously they don’t.

Cabin was neat and comfortable, good facilities however cabin could have been cleaner and needs treatment for ants in the kitchen. Beds were fairly comfortable and shower head was amazing! Had a bunch of uni students in the pool area with alcohol which made it less than appealing for our young child. Basic buffet breakfast onsite was yummy! TVs weren’t smart so couldn’t do any streaming etc but wifi was good.

As a family we have been staying here each year, at the same time of the year for the past 10+ years.  Has always been a pleasant stay while exploring Canberra.  This year was quoted $95 a night for a simple drive through site for 2 people only, no kids.  You have to be joking.  You've lost my business indefinitely.  Read the owners reply.  The pool is cold can't use it and don't need a camp kitchen.  Last year l paid $65 a night for a drive through site this year $95 to park in a drive thru site o/night is outrageous.  Owners reply is not factually correct.

I can't comment on cabins as we stayed in our van, but what a great park! Lovely and quiet, despite tons of kids being around, really well maintained grounds and facilities, and the restaurant was better than I expected!. We will definitely be staying here next time we are in Canberra.

Been coming to this place around the same time every year. This time, 24 August 2024, it was not a pleasant experience. The cabin given to us smelled of urine and the corners and beams were full of cobwebs. There were cockroaches crawling on the wall and on the curtains. Some curtains were dirty and ripped. The smell of urine was sickening. I reported it to this man at reception . He said he will change our cabin and asked us to see him at reception. When I got there to return the key cards, he said he already sent housekeeping to fix the problem. He asked that we go back to the cabin.So I told him that he told me that he was going to give us another cabin. He looked hesitant to do that but I told him what he told me - to take our belongings as we are going to another cabin. I felt like he was trying to trick me by sending housekeeping to fix the issue. Well, we got into the other cabin, and was a bit better. Just a little bit better. The bunkbed mattresses have no covers. The shower curtain was very much stained with brownish colour. The next day our keycards didn't work. We had to have them reprogrammed. The restaurant was not as good as it used to be. Staff were grumpy, menu was limited and kitchen closed early. Their reason was there are not too many people. Breakfast was almost gone and not replenished. Check out time I guess was the best time as we were leaving and have decided not to ever go back there ever again. The same man receptionist who also works as barista didn't even ask how our stay was. I guess he knew the answer if he asked. 4 straight years we've been coming to this place. This year was a big disappointment. By the way, the roll of toilet paper was less than 1cm thick when we got there.

What a wonderful caravan park.  Staff were so friendly and helpful.  Caravan sites are all drive through and easy to access. So much for kids to do.  Perfect location. Nestled in the bush but so close to everything.  Will definitely come neck again

The worst overpriced place we have stayed while travelling around the eastern states. The customer service was appalling with no care at all. We got locked out of our cabin because somebody failed to check our booking after the first night even although  we were booked for three nights.
The cabin was old and no room to get into the main bed with about 600mm room around the bed. The bathroom was that small the you had to get dressed outside. There are no power outlets in the seating area and we had to run an extension lead out to a power board.
Cleaning has a lot to be desired.There was not enough plates or cultery for the cabin and what was there was all mismated and old.
We were offered to upgrade to another cabin at $40.00 per night so the would make it $248.00 per night for two people...what a joke.
The management is more worried about corporate bookings than
family people .

Room was a great size and layout but was not cleaned well and felt gross sitting on heavily marked lounges with rubbish under cushions etc.
There were big sticky patches on the tiles, rotting apple under the kids bed, food splatters on kitchen cupboards, rubbish in the lounge and everything just felt gross,

On our first night we noticed none of the 4 smoke alarms had batteries, notified reception at 5pm and didn’t hear from anyone until we called again at 7.20pm, when a manager dropped off a single battery and said that should do til morning!. We asked for more batteries and installed them to have alarms continuously beep and wake our young children as something wasn’t right.
It was well over an hour of googling and sorting the issue out ourselves, to find a damaged alarm was setting the rest off.

By this point we had 4 kids asleep and 2 distressed and exhausted after a day of travel, we were told we could move all of our stuff to another villa with working alarms if we wanted. Thankfully my husband was able to take a working alarm from the empty villa and replace the broken one.

A very disappointing and stressful first night!

We had to constantly chase for clean towels, had no dishwasher tablets or garbage bags left in the room etc, and there were ants everywhere that spoiled food in the kitchen and were even crawling all over the bedding in one of the kids rooms (probably because there was rotten fruit left under the bed!).

All in all it wasn’t very relaxing and I definitely wouldn’t stay again.

I did try to discuss issues with staff at the time and was handed a business card and told to email my feedback, they’ve conveniently not replied to the last 2 emails, so I’m interested to see if they reply to reviews!

Stayed 5 years ago and had a great experience. Last week – URGH!!! Thought we were doing well when villa was available at 1pm. However, couldn’t get car anywhere close to unpack because of a 4wd + trailer parked across all assigned villa parking. Took management until 6pm and multiple guest complaints to take action. Then we were greeted by thick possum poo, cockroaches, toothpaste down walls, crumbs every where and a bed with no mattress protector and a stained mattress ++. Asked to move; better, but greeted at the door with an empty deodorant bottle and half used cake of soap. Inside grease drips down outside of oven, splashes over cupboards, utensils with thick dried food on them, crumbs inside multiple cupboards and mouldy grout in the shower floor. And a TV which asked every 10mins to allow a device (we never worked out what) to connect to the WiFi hub. The “fully equipped kitchen” had no oven or dishwasher – well not even a dish tray for that matter.
Of course no water on our first morning!
Scuffs had black souls when we left after 5 days from ground in dirt on floor & $1300 poorer.
All understandable when you see the cleaners walking between villas with a small bucket, mop and vacuum to do a quick minimal flick clean between guests.
Management had packed more cabins/villas in, reduced the length of parking spots to be inadequate for even a medium sized car and seemed to be catering more for hundreds of school children.
On exit they said “looking forward to seeing you again” – NOT LIKELY!
We stayed in a caravan with on suit nice park close to CBD

Super close to GIO stadium
Excellent restaurant and facilities
Could probably do with extra staff in peak times

Alivio Tourist Park is next level. We stayed in a Garden Villa. 2 bed room. Fantastic space. Clean. There is plenty of room. The whole park is fantastic. Very clean and well maintained. So many activities for the kids. Own restaurant serving breakfasts and dinners. 100% recommend.

The bathroom floors and cupboards had not been cleaned. The TV only had 6 channels and some music channels. No Foxtel or Smart connections available. The service at front counter was below average. It was advertised as the spa being all year round as its heated but it was empty. Very poor playgrounds for children considering the size of the Park really could have alot more for children to do. The bed was great and kitchen space good but overall I would not stay again and would not recommend for a family to stay at.

3rd time staying at AlivioTourist Park this time $4000 plus for 10 nights on arrival was told pool and spa not in working order no warning prior to arrival date,stains on mattresses  pillowcases on clean days room
Cleaners don’t change pillowcases or single beds at all ,hole
Behind front door where door handle hits wall,this  tourist location has definitely gone down hill fast compared to 1st visit

Cabin was small and filthy. Ended up leaving after 1 night. Worst family holiday ever. Food was alright but after one meal decided to eat in a nice Canberra restaurant. Cocktail hour was good. Wouldn’t recommend rather hotel
Didn't like the food and the roads are too narrow for big caravans

We stayed here for a few days for a little getaway . The older 'ridge' cabin was not so great, beds were horrible and it was quite old and freezing!! After giving some feedback to the staff the next day they were able to upgrade our room for a great price and they informed us that the older cabins were next to get fixed up! We then stayed in a newer villa which was great, beds were comfy and the place was new and clean!
Don't rate the restaurant or the breakfast buffet but the staff all around the park were very nice and had great customer service!

Great facilities. Pool, Spa etc.

Rooms are quite cold in Winter. 4 bunks in one little room but the other double bed room is quite sizable.

They know how to charge. Washing and dryer is about $12. Cabin for about $1400 for 5 days.

Great staff
We have been to this Park twice and stayed in a cabin We love it. We'll placed in Canberra. Clean. Great on-site restaurant. Great leisure facilities. Highly recommended.

How this place has negative reviews I have no idea. The room (Garden Villa) was immaculate upon arrival (ok except one plastic water bottle left on the window ceil) and everything was perfectly clean and tidy. The park’s facilities are really cool and the kids had a great time for the one arvo we spent here as part of an overnight stop on our road trip. Highly recommend!

We stayed in the newer cabins which were great but the shower floor had some slimey yellow stuff in places that was pretty gross. The resturant said the wait would be 30mins. After an hour and paying $49 for pork ribs,chips and salad I was less than impressed. The ribs were dry, tough and half had no sauce. The salad was basic with no dressing option. After such a long wait and at that price point I expected alot more. Great place if you bring your own food.
Great pool and amenities.

Overall the cabin was the perfect size for our family of 3 kids, location was perfect short drive to shops restaurants attractions,  was tidy enough the only thing I have point out was the toilet smells like pee,  and the cleaners had forgotten to clean under the single bed in kids room my children found two beer bottles under the single bed.

Great spot and facilities. Cabins are a good size, with a kitchen. Unfortunately the floor of the cabin was filthy and the bathroom floor had long black hair on the floor. I swept it myself but it needed a good mop as well.

This is a great park when travelling for vacation and the drive-through van sites are super convenient.  I've stayed a few times, most recently in a cabin.  Well appointed with great facilities for all interests.  Will stay again when travelling for holidays.

Only drawback? It was impossible to get mobile data reception.  Unfeasible to write reports after a day's work.  The good news is there are several libraries open to 8pm within 15 mins drive where you can grab a desk and a reliable connection including National Library of Australia.

$6 coffee price was a bit of a shock too.

Great place. Good location. A lot of activities within the park. Clean accommodation. I stayed in the Ridge View Cabin for reference. The only downside was that you could hear the main road in the morning and evening.

Awesome park, caravan sights are all drive through and well spaced. Heaps for kids to do! Amenities are clean and modern. Nice and close to the city. Definitely wouldn't order the takeaway pizza with garlic bread, would be better off going to the supermarket and getting a frozen pizza and garlic bread. It was almost uneatable and $25, $26 pizzas with $9 garlic bread. Other than that I can't fault the park all staff were so welcoming and friendly.

Edit: The front counter was contacted on the night and informed me she would pass on to management. We never received a response.

rooms are clean and functional. the caravan park is not like a traditionsl campground, it was mire likena retreat nicw paved roads in a bush setting i noticwd some people towing caravans but not sure if there are grass areas for putting up a tent. gteat f9e kids, my kids loved the basketball court  chalk graffiti board, tennis court and play ground. good oub style restaurant too, we got pizzas but i saw someone ordered a chickwn snitzel meal at i looked massive.
I stayed in a caravan, so the one star review was for the camp kitchen. No heating in temperatures between -2 degrees to 9 degrees is no fun. PUT IN SOME HEATING!
Nice enough a place. The rooms are a descent size and were reasonably clean. Did not have to engage with the staff much due to a short stay but they seemed friendly. The buffet breakfast was good.
Great little hidden secret.  Check it out if you get the chance.

We were so comfortable in our Boronia Villa. The whole place was very homely. The beds comfortable. Cosy on mornings when it was minus 2 degrees. Very private. The Verandah cafe was perfect for our family reunion dinners. So convenient to all the city attractions and our relatives. The staff very friendly and helpful. We will be back!

I’ve never left a place early due to hating it.
I stayed a week. Cut short by a night because I couldn’t handle it once my business In Canberra was done.
This was only going to a be a 3 star review because I immediately thought the place was not 4 stars on arrival. However it’s a 1 due to how bad it was.

Room wasn’t cleaned once in the week I stayed even after I asked about it. Instead staff entered the room without notifying me that same evening (said they’d clean it the next day) and made the bed.
That’s not cleaning. Also the lack of notifying me. I had my valuables left out including a very expensive laptop.

First cabin was missing a curtain because according to staff: they change them every year due to mold build up and forgot to replace that one. So I moved cabins.

Second cabin had a dangerous trip hazard directly outside. I tripped hard on the first night and still tripped on it a few times after because you can’t see it in the dark.

One of the windows in the cabin also had no lock.

The place in general was very dark on the grounds and poorly lit when walking from the restaurant to the further away cabins. With cars moving in that same dark space.

So overall, multiple safety issues.

Aside from the safety concerns the cabin was tiny, the internal doors didn’t stay open and constantly closed, very annoying when trying to move around the cabin. I had to stick toilet paper under them.

The bed was one of the worst I have slept on. Very uncomfortable, ancient mattress, stains on the quilt. Lack of bedding. It was literally a sheet, a quilt with no cover in-between two more sheets. That’s it.
Every other place I’ve stayed has always had extra blankets in the wardrobe.
In Canberra in winter especially would think they’d have extra blankets. But no.

The restaurant was terrible. I ate there most nights and didn’t finish most of my meals. Expensive too. Staff seemed unhappy, on one busy night I think a Saturday I saw management having a go at staff. No wonder they seemed unhappy. They were all trying their best though.
The ribs especially were terrible. And the most expensive item on the menu. The “sticky bbq sauce” was extremely watery.
The salad/slaw that came as a side with most mains was foul.
The schnitzel came with the most stingy tiny serving of gravy, not enough for the meal making the already dry schnitzel incredibly dry.
The only decent meal was the steak which was surprisingly cooked great. However again, the sauce, absolutely tiny serving.

The aircon also started started making a scary noise and had to be turned off at like 11pm on one night. That made for a freezing night and morning. They actually fixed that when I complained though. Didn’t clean the room though which was the second time I asked about it when I complained about the aircon.

The parking outside the cabins was really small. I purposely chose this place over a private apartment because I had a large 4wd with me and needed space for it. It barely fit at the first cabin and was a bit better at the second.

Also weirdly, every morning my car was covered in rat droppings. Like completely covered. I didn’t see any rats but I know the droppings when I see them.

No refund on early check out even with travel insurance. Whatever though for that one.

This is one of the worst places I’ve stayed. Never again.

our cabin was absolutely filthy. Dirty floors, linen stained with who knows what, 30 year old mattresses and lounges held together with timber to stop you falling through the cushions, a dip on the floor and piles of dust , pringles left in the lounge from a pervious guest but the absolute icing was finding a pervious guess used UNDERWAEAR under our bed .. brought it up with management and they were extremely apologetic and gave us a free breakfast for it and handled it well  .. but the cleaning is really dismal and disgusting
Cabin new and clean.  Well laid out.  Excellent play facilities.  Close enough to all the tourist locations.
Enjoyed our stay clean and tidy

Close to the city.
Great rooms and facilities
It's now our go to for trips to Canberra

Great caravan park in bush setting close to Canberra City and Belconnen.
Nice and quiet.

Stayed n the Grevillea cabin,  pleasant rooms.  Just need a few more items in the kitchen, breadboard, colander, extra cups.  Also an under blanket or just a mattress protector on the bed would make it a bit warmer and hygienic.  Very quiet at night.
Great place to stay clean up beat vibe

We had an excellent first experience at Alivio and will definitely be back!
The Verandah was a stand out and we ate/drank here both nights and mornings of our stay. From the service to the quality of food and available entertainment for our kids (even the pencils for colouring in were all freshly sharpened!) this establishment has all the hallmarks of perfect management.
The place is great overall, but the pool uses a bit much chlorine then I would like.
Lovely park with great facilities. Peaceful location. Staff provided a great service. Highly recommend.
Great location, nestled amongst the trees. Cabin was clean and well equipped for our family of four.

Grounds are immaculate. The cabin however was not so good. Mould in the bathroom. Cabin smelt like sweat. The furniture needs replacing, the double bed comforter, and covering up the old mattress is broken. The lounge is broken and dirty from years of use, and the kitchen chairs are no better. Was not worth what we paid. And also we were given 2 face washers for 4 people and 2 toilet rolls for 4 days and 4 people. This place seems to be going down hill.

Great place for the family very close to everything and the facilities and rooms very clean.  Restaurant was great and the staff very knowledgeable about the local offerings of gin! Some of the little things that stood out for us were the play areas all in a nice central location and combined with the traffic flow made it easy for the kids to wonder off to play. Our room was the 3 room villa which is great for extended family as there was a seperate room with an ensuite. 10/10

This location and facilities are on point. The cabins are clean, the playgrounds, sporting courts, barbecue areas are great.  The customer service is 10/10
The thing that pulls it down is breakfast buffet. Although selection of  is great, the supposed hot food seems always to be lukewarm to cold - whether we enter upon opening or an hour later.  We stayed there for 4 breakfast days.   The self poured coffee does not have stirrers anywhere near it and the toaster only toasts one side.
These are minor details but should really be addressed

Came to Canberra for an overnight stop, for the Ralph Heimans Portrait exhibition at the National Portait Gallery. Decided on Alivio Tourist Park, due to its near proximity to Canberra CBD.
Very pleased. Warmly welcomed. Cabin close to Reception area. Given a Park map, instructed how to get to our cabin. Cabin, neat clean. All set up as we wanted. Couldn't asked for more.
Best place to stay in Canberra. Very close to city centre and tons of recreational activities with in facility including a pool.
Best caravan park ever
Best holiday spot ever

Just want to say from me and the other brothers that were up there. The only bad thing that I can say is that there wasn’t enough toilet paper in the rooms other than that the friendly service, the rooms themselves and the cleanliness of the place was bloody amazing. If I could give it more than five stars, I would
We camped and the service was fantastic, great central location. Our ensuite bathroom was very clean and convenient. Highly recommend.
Need to clean glass doors & glass panels on patio.
It was great.But ... The cortens were riped.

5 star caravan park/resort.

This park is more of a resort with its luxury offerings such as bar, restaurant, amenities and set up.

We had a very nice stay. My only suggestion would be to please install heaters for the main pool. It's bloody cold and I was in there on a 28 degree day.
We had a lovely stay it was just too short! Will be staying longer next time!!

Very out of the way caravan park.
Place located on a slope. Has a pool. Nice communal bathroom. Has an eatery that's opened 6 nights a week ( closed Sundays)
Lots of concrete. Some cabins have electric vehicle chargers.

We've travelled 13,000km in the past three months and this was our most expensive stay by far at $89 per night for a powered site. The site was very nice though access was a little tight and we were very close to our neighbours. The reception staff could work harder at conveying a warm, personal welcome. Facilities were modern and clean. We asked for a late check-out and it was refused. Against policy apparently. I don't understand the logic.

I would give 0 stars if i could.
Where to begin! Firstly, we booked this caravan park for the splash park (we have 3 very young children, the eldest being 3 and one with a disability).
When we arrived and went to check it out, to my horror it was out of order. When i went to reception to ask about it, the receptionist said it had only been closed for 2 days, however a lady standing next to me said she had been at the park for over a week and it had been closed the whole time.... (so somebody is lying....)
The receptionist said they have other things for children like boardgames and movies to hire, as well as basketball and tennis courts.... I told her the ages of children (my 3 year old is hardly interested in tennis and my 1 year old will just eat the boardgame) and that its 35 degrees all week, we booked for the splash park.
When I asked for some renumeration, considering I had called earlier that week to confirm our booking and no one said anything about the splash park being out of order and nothing was mentioned on the website, the guy who i spoke to (I believe his name was Ahmed), showed no compassion or understanding for the situation and just fobbed me off trying to placate me with a free snack as if I were an angry raccoon. This place clearly does not care about customer service or the guests at the park. Your comfort means nothing once they've deducted the money from your account prior to your stay.
The cabin itself, apart from being much smaller than it looks online was ok. Clearly hadn't been thoroughly cleaned as when picking up my childrens toy from underneath the couch, I found the previous guests old muffin.....
HOWEVER, the airon is an absolute JOKE! Did not work and did not cool down the cabin at all, it was like a sauna in there.  We ended up leaving 2 days earlier than planned. Extremely unimpressed.

The area around the cabin is nice, nestled around the bush but its not nice enough to make up for the 0 courtesy or care for the guests or the pathetic aircon in the rooms. Spend your money elsewhere.

This is such an amazing park, outstanding facilities for the kids with a resort style pool, basketball courts, volleyball courts, tennis courts and so much more! The deck overlooking the pool is great for the adults to sit and relax whilst watching the kids, plus a bar and restaurant which means everything you need is at the park! Plenty of cabin options and drive through powered sites with and without ensuites. Mountain bike tracks everywhere and an easy ride into the city to see the sites like Parliament house and the war memorial. Highly recommend.
Nice place to stay, even if it was over night.

We were on a weekend getaway for a family members 40th birthday. I wasn’t sure what to expect as I’ve never stayed in a tourist park before. I was so pleasantly surprised at just how set up it was for the kids. We were a large group and the staff were so helpful by putting all our cabins next to each other. They also reserved tables for the party. The cabins were quite dated but we knew what we were booking and the price reflects that. Everything we needed as a family of 4 with a baby. Set on the outskirts of town surrounded by bush land. Incredible pool and splash park area for the kids. Beautiful big verandah for dining/hanging out for a drink while you watch the kids in the pool. Couldn’t fault it as a family experience!

We hebben een nacht in een Cabin geslapen tegenover de receptie. Ze waren erg oud, niet goed schoon en de vloerbedekking in de slaapkamer was ronduit vies. Alles was slecht schoongemaakt, overal stof, vlekken in de gordijnen en spinnenwebben binnen. De cabines zouden vervangen moeten worden voor een nieuwer exemplaar.
Het park zag er verder wel mooi uit.

This is a lovely tree-filled park near city attractions with a restaurant and nicer than average laundry room.  We reserved an "accessible" room online.  We have stayed in numerous holiday parks throughout Australia and this is the only one that did not include a shower with a seat and grab bars or bars near the toilet.  The bathroom was large and open with a glass wall separating the shower area (no door) but there was nothing to hold on to.  I did inquire and was told that the only accessible accommodation they are required to make is a ramp entry, which we had. That may be true but this was the only time in our experience accessible lodging didn't include bathroom accommodation.   One other weird thing was that the king bed turned out to be twins pushed together with a big gap and no mattress pad/cover. When I asked after discovering this after one of two nights I was told the mattresses should zip together (I looked later and didn't see any zippers).  To be fair, they offered to send housekeeping to remake the bed which I declined.  My advice for anyone booking would be to check details that might be important to you.  We did not do this in advance as we've never encountered quirks like this before.

Clean bathroom.4 showers and 3 toilets.
Has heat lamps on timer for winter.
Plenty of families here.
Lots of concrete and drive  through sites for vans.
Breakfast open 7 days 7:30-10:30.
Dinners 6 days a week Monday to Saturday.
Not a great place to stay if only overnight heading south  as takes around 40 minutes to get back onto Monaro highway.
Has electric car chargers outside larger cabins. Has hire electric scooters out front and swimming pool.
Wi fi worked in some places  , Telstra phone reception  didn't work for data.

Staff were friendly on check in.
Room was clean enough although a dirty sock was found in one of the beds.
The beds were very uncomfortable especially on the bunks - You could feel every slat through the mattress. The others were not quite as bad but were far from comfortable
Lovely camp grounds, very well maintained and modern facilities. Little bit on the expensive side for just a regular camper space but atleast there is nice facilities included.

Great spot so close to the city but surrounded in bushland! We took our van and loved the drive through van spots. The pool was awesome and so good having a restaurant and bar there. A fantastic stay. We will be back!!

Excellent stay for four nights to catch up with family.
The pool was well used by grandchildren, followed by pizza on the deck.
Staff were friendly and accommodating.

Room was a bit old and tied but served my purpose. Location was fantastic, I was on a mountain bike trip and it was conveniently located with trails right outside the door. A short drive to Mt Stromlo and surrounding tracks made it the perfect spot to crash at night
Having the restaurant and bar on site was fabulous, the beer was cold and the food great. All round an enjoyable trip.

A fantastic hidden gem in Canberra. The park is very child and family friendly with pools, basketball, ping pong and playgrounds. The buffet breakfast was perfect to send you on y it r way. Highly recommend.
